commit.message:
refactor(web): relocate seo.ts from lib/ to app/_seo.ts — fix W1 (A1-PR4) (#1128)

## Summary
- Fixes the remaining W1 violation `lib/seo.ts → @/theme/brand-assets`
by relocating `seo.ts` up one layer (lib → app).
- `seo.ts` is page-metadata logic exclusive to the App Router — all 4
callers already live under `src/app/`. Moving the module is simpler than
restructuring `theme/`.
- Baseline shrinks **17 → 16**. W6 (`theme/brand-themes →
lib/auth/types`) stays in baseline for a separate small PR.

## Why relocate instead of restructuring theme/

Reviewer-suggested approach.

| Scope | This PR (relocate `seo.ts`) | Alternative (split
`theme/brand-*` → `config/brand/*`) |
|---|---|---|
| Files touched | **7** | ~23 |
| Diff | **+5 / −14** | +31 / −49 |
| Violations fixed | W1 | W1 + W6 |
| theme/ structure | unchanged | rewritten |

The alternative required relocating 2 files from `theme/` plus 16 caller
import rewrites. This PR only moves `seo.ts` + its test and updates 4
callers. W6 is a 1-line fix (move `STORAGE_KEYS.BRAND_THEME` out of
`lib/auth/types`) deferred to its own PR.

## Why `@/app/_seo`

`src/app/` forbids top consumer imports from nothing (app is Layer 4),
so `app → theme` is allowed by W1-W6. Leading underscore matches the
existing `src/app/[locale]/_presignedFactory.ts` convention — Next.js
ignores underscore-prefixed non-route files.

Alternative homes considered + rejected:
- `src/app/seo.ts` — no underscore convention in this codebase
- `src/lib/app-metadata/seo.ts` — still `lib/`, W1 still fails
- `src/app/[locale]/_seo.ts` — locks it under [locale]; `app/robots.ts`
and `app/layout.tsx` are root-level

## Changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `web/src/lib/seo.ts` | `git mv` → `web/src/app/_seo.ts` |
| `web/tests/unit/lib/seo.unit.spec.ts` | `git mv` →
`web/tests/unit/app/_seo.unit.spec.ts` |
| `web/src/app/[locale]/about/page.tsx` | import `@/lib/seo` →
`@/app/_seo` |
| `web/src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx` | same + import sort reshuffled |
| `web/src/app/layout.tsx` | same |
| `web/src/app/robots.ts` | same |
| `web/.dependency-cruiser-known-violations.json` | remove W1 seo entry
(17 → 16) |

## Local verification
- `pnpm lint:imports` — exit 0, \`16 known violations ignored\`
- `pnpm test:unit tests/unit/app/_seo.unit.spec.ts` — 6 tests pass
- `npx tsc --noEmit` — clean
- `pnpm lint` — clean

## Remaining baseline 16

After this PR lands: W2(3) / W3(5) / W4(2) / W5(4) / W6(2) — fully
UI-and-feature-isolation cluster + 2 theme-leaf entries (brand-themes →
lib/auth/types + brand-assets → theme internal). Each subsequent cleanup
PR removes a handful.

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests pass (6 tests)
- [x] dep-cruiser hard gate still green (17 → 16 known violations)
- [x] tsc + eslint clean
- [ ] CI confirms web-quality + asset-size-guard + jscpd
- [ ] Reviewer validates `_seo` underscore naming + relocation approach

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
test(web): DiagnosticsSection 全面覆盖 (#894 Step 5 补) (#1129)

## Summary

Epic #894 Step 5 (#899) — 再补一个：\`DiagnosticsSection.tsx\` (338 LOC) 从 0%
→ 全分支。49 tests.

## 源码变动（极小）

- 6 个 pure helper (parseCpu/Memory/Disk + formatCpu/Memory/Disk) 加
export
- 零语义变化、零 runtime 行为变化

## 新增 49 个测试

| 分组 | # | 覆盖 |
|---|---|---|
| parseCpuMillicores | 4 | 空串 / nanocores / millicores / 整核 |
| parseMemoryMiB | 7 | 空串 / Gi/Mi/Ki (binary) / G/M (decimal) / 裸 bytes
|
| parseDiskGiB | 5 | 空串 / Gi / Mi/Ki 降级 / 裸 bytes |
| formatCpu | 4 | 空串 / >= 1000ms / 中段 / sub-milli |
| formatMemory | 4 | 空串 / >= 1024 / 中段 / sub-MiB |
| formatDisk | 4 | 空串 / Gi / Mi / Ki |
| DiagnosticsSection gate | 2 | !botRunning → 提示 / true → Card 渲染 |
| ResourcesCard | 10 | loading/error/empty/data 状态机 / bot_id & pod_name
可选 / disk gauge 有无 / usage=undefined 提示 / refresh 回调 / SemiCircleGauge
>=80% destructive / <80% success / 0% → "—" |
| LogsViewer | 6 | loading/empty/logs / Tail select default=100 + change
传给 refresh / Timestamps checkbox / loading 按钮 disabled |

## Bug-hunting

无新发现。状态机清晰；gauge 阈值切换（80%）逻辑对；parse/format 单位处理完整。

## Test plan

- [x] 49/49 通过
- [x] tsc clean
- [x] lint clean
- [ ] CI 绿

## 关联

- Epic #894 Step 5 (#899)
- 剩余：ConnectorsSection (493) / ClawSettingsClient (438 关键 ~120)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,361 @@
+// @vitest-environment jsdom
+import { cleanup, fireEvent, render } from '@testing-library/react'
+import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest'
+
+import type { BotResourcesResponse, ContainerResources } from '@/lib/api/openclaw-settings'
+
+// ── Hoisted mocks ──
+
+const hoisted = vi.hoisted(() => ({
+  useTranslation: vi.fn(),
+}))
+
+vi.mock('@/contexts/LanguageContext', () => ({
+  useTranslation: hoisted.useTranslation,
+}))
+
+import {
+  DiagnosticsSection,
+  formatCpu,
+  formatDisk,
+  formatMemory,
+  LogsViewer,
+  parseCpuMillicores,
+  parseDiskGiB,
+  parseMemoryMiB,
+  ResourcesCard,
+} from '@/app/[locale]/claw-settings/components/DiagnosticsSection'
+
+// ── Fixtures ──
+
+function buildContainer(overrides: Partial<ContainerResources> = {}): ContainerResources {
+  return {
+    requests: { cpu: '100m', memory: '128Mi' },
+    limits: { cpu: '500m', memory: '512Mi' },
+    usage: { cpu: '250m', memory: '256Mi' },
+    ...overrides,
+  }
+}
+
+function buildResources(overrides: Partial<BotResourcesResponse> = {}): BotResourcesResponse {
+  return {
+    bot_id: 'bot-123',
+    pod_name: 'pod-abc',
+    containers: { main: buildContainer() },
+    ...overrides,
+  }
+}
+
+describe('DiagnosticsSection', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    hoisted.useTranslation.mockReturnValue((key: string) => key)
+  })
+
+  afterEach(() => {
+    cleanup()
+  })
+
+  // ── Pure helpers ──
+  describe('parseCpuMillicores', () => {
+    it('"" → 0', () => {
+      expect(parseCpuMillicores('')).toBe(0)
+    })
+    it('nanocores "500000000n" → 500ms', () => {
+      expect(parseCpuMillicores('500000000n')).toBe(500)
+    })
+    it('millicores "250m" → 250', () => {
+      expect(parseCpuMillicores('250m')).toBe(250)
+    })
+    it('whole cores "2" → 2000ms', () => {
+      expect(parseCpuMillicores('2')).toBe(2000)
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe('parseMemoryMiB', () => {
+    it('"" → 0', () => {
+      expect(parseMemoryMiB('')).toBe(0)
+    })
+    it('"2Gi" → 2048 MiB', () => {
+      expect(parseMemoryMiB('2Gi')).toBe(2048)
+    })
+    it('"512Mi" → 512 MiB', () => {
+      expect(parseMemoryMiB('512Mi')).toBe(512)
+    })
+    it('"1024Ki" → 1 MiB', () => {
+      expect(parseMemoryMiB('1024Ki')).toBe(1)
+    })
+    it('"2G" (decimal G) → 2000 MiB', () => {
+      expect(parseMemoryMiB('2G')).toBe(2000)
+    })
+    it('"128M" (decimal M) → 128 MiB', () => {
+      expect(parseMemoryMiB('128M')).toBe(128)
+    })
+    it('raw bytes → divided by (1024*1024)', () => {
+      expect(parseMemoryMiB('1048576')).toBe(1)
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe('parseDiskGiB', () => {
+    it('"" → 0', () => {
+      expect(parseDiskGiB('')).toBe(0)
+    })
+    it('"5Gi" → 5 GiB', () => {
+      expect(parseDiskGiB('5Gi')).toBe(5)
+    })
+    it('"512Mi" → 0.5 GiB', () => {
+      expect(parseDiskGiB('512Mi')).toBe(0.5)
+    })
+    it('"1024Ki" → ~0.00095 GiB', () => {
+      expect(parseDiskGiB('1024Ki')).toBeCloseTo(1 / 1024, 6)
+    })
+    it('raw bytes → divided by 1024^3', () => {
+      expect(parseDiskGiB(String(1024 * 1024 * 1024))).toBe(1)
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe('formatCpu', () => {
+    it('"" → "—"', () => {
+      expect(formatCpu('')).toBe('—')
+    })
+    it('>= 1000ms → "X.X cores"', () => {
+      expect(formatCpu('2500m')).toBe('2.5 cores')
+    })
+    it('>= 1ms, < 1000ms → "{N}m" rounded', () => {
+      expect(formatCpu('250m')).toBe('250m')
+    })
+    it('sub-milli (e.g. 0.5m from 500000n) → "0.5m" one decimal', () => {
+      expect(formatCpu('500000n')).toBe('0.5m')
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe('formatMemory', () => {
+    it('"" → "—"', () => {
+      expect(formatMemory('')).toBe('—')
+    })
+    it('>= 1024 MiB → "X.X Gi"', () => {
+      expect(formatMemory('2048Mi')).toBe('2.0 Gi')
+    })
+    it('>= 1 MiB, < 1024 → "{N} Mi"', () => {
+      expect(formatMemory('256Mi')).toBe('256 Mi')
+    })
+    it('sub-MiB → "{N} Ki"', () => {
+      expect(formatMemory('512Ki')).toBe('512 Ki')
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe('formatDisk', () => {
+    it('"" → "—"', () => {
+      expect(formatDisk('')).toBe('—')
+    })
+    it('>= 1 GiB → "X.X Gi"', () => {
+      expect(formatDisk('5Gi')).toBe('5.0 Gi')
+    })
+    it('< 1 GiB, >= 1 MiB → "X.X Mi"', () => {
+      expect(formatDisk('512Mi')).toBe('512.0 Mi')
+    })
+    it('< 1 MiB → "X.X Ki"', () => {
+      expect(formatDisk('512Ki')).toBe('512.0 Ki')
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── DiagnosticsSection gate ──
+  describe('DiagnosticsSection botRunning gate', () => {
+    const noopProps = {
+      logs: null,
+      logsLoading: false,
+      resources: null,
+      resourcesLoading: false,
+      resourcesError: null,
+      onFetchLogs: vi.fn(async () => {}),
+      onFetchResources: vi.fn(async () => {}),
+    }
+
+    it('botRunning=false → shows "Bot must be running" message only', () => {
+      const { getByText, queryByText } = render(<DiagnosticsSection {...noopProps} botRunning={false} />)
+      expect(getByText('settings.botMustBeRunning')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(queryByText('settings.resources')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(queryByText('settings.logs')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('botRunning=true → renders both ResourcesCard and LogsViewer', () => {
+      const { getByText } = render(<DiagnosticsSection {...noopProps} botRunning />)
+      expect(getByText('settings.resources')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('settings.logs')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── ResourcesCard ──
+  describe('ResourcesCard', () => {
+    const baseProps = {
+      resources: null,
+      loading: false,
+      error: null as string | null,
+      onRefresh: vi.fn(),
+    }
+
+    it('loading + no resources → shows loading text + disables Refresh', () => {
+      const { getByText } = render(<ResourcesCard {...baseProps} loading />)
+      expect(getByText('common.loading')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      // Refresh button shows "Refreshing..." label and disabled=true
+      const refresh = getByText('settings.refreshing') as HTMLButtonElement
+      expect(refresh).toBeDisabled()
+    })
+
+    it('error set → shows error message', () => {
+      const { getByText } = render(<ResourcesCard {...baseProps} error="Kube API 500" />)
+      expect(getByText('Kube API 500')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('no resources and not loading → "noDataAvailable"', () => {
+      const { getByText } = render(<ResourcesCard {...baseProps} />)
+      expect(getByText('settings.noDataAvailable')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('resources → renders bot_id + pod_name + container gauges', () => {
+      const { getByText } = render(<ResourcesCard {...baseProps} resources={buildResources()} />)
+      expect(getByText('bot-123')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('pod-abc')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('CPU')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('Memory')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('resources.bot_id empty → Bot ID line hidden', () => {
+      const resources = buildResources({ bot_id: '' })
+      const { queryByText } = render(<ResourcesCard {...baseProps} resources={resources} />)
+      expect(queryByText(/Bot ID/)).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('resources.pod_name empty → Identifier line hidden', () => {
+      const resources = buildResources({ pod_name: '' })
+      const { queryByText } = render(<ResourcesCard {...baseProps} resources={resources} />)
+      expect(queryByText(/Identifier/)).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('container WITH disk limit → Disk gauge rendered', () => {
+      const c = buildContainer({
+        limits: { cpu: '500m', memory: '512Mi', disk: '10Gi' },
+        usage: { cpu: '100m', memory: '256Mi', disk: '2Gi' },
+      })
+      const { getByText } = render(
+        <ResourcesCard {...baseProps} resources={buildResources({ containers: { main: c } })} />,
+      )
+      expect(getByText('Disk')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('container WITHOUT disk limit → Disk gauge hidden', () => {
+      const c = buildContainer({
+        limits: { cpu: '500m', memory: '512Mi' },
+        usage: { cpu: '100m', memory: '256Mi' },
+      })
+      const { queryByText } = render(
+        <ResourcesCard {...baseProps} resources={buildResources({ containers: { main: c } })} />,
+      )
+      expect(queryByText('Disk')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('container with NO usage data → "usage data not available" note', () => {
+      const c = buildContainer({ usage: undefined })
+      const { getByText } = render(
+        <ResourcesCard {...baseProps} resources={buildResources({ containers: { main: c } })} />,
+      )
+      expect(getByText('settings.usageDataNotAvailable')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('click Refresh (not loading) → onRefresh fires', () => {
+      const onRefresh = vi.fn()
+      const { getByText } = render(<ResourcesCard {...baseProps} onRefresh={onRefresh} />)
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('common.refresh'))
+      expect(onRefresh).toHaveBeenCalledOnce()
+    })
+
+    it('SemiCircleGauge percent >= 80% → uses destructive stroke class', () => {
+      const c = buildContainer({
+        limits: { cpu: '100m', memory: '100Mi' },
+        usage: { cpu: '90m', memory: '90Mi' }, // 90% usage
+      })
+      const { container } = render(
+        <ResourcesCard {...baseProps} resources={buildResources({ containers: { main: c } })} />,
+      )
+      // At least one destructive stroke on the gauge arc
+      expect(container.querySelector('.stroke-destructive')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('SemiCircleGauge percent < 80% → uses success stroke class', () => {
+      const c = buildContainer({
+        limits: { cpu: '1000m', memory: '1000Mi' },
+        usage: { cpu: '100m', memory: '100Mi' }, // 10% usage
+      })
+      const { container } = render(
+        <ResourcesCard {...baseProps} resources={buildResources({ containers: { main: c } })} />,
+      )
+      expect(container.querySelector('.stroke-ecap-success-text')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(container.querySelector('.stroke-destructive')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('SemiCircleGauge percent = 0 → dashes across all rendered gauges, no filled arc', () => {
+      const c = buildContainer({ usage: { cpu: '', memory: '' } })
+      const { container } = render(
+        <ResourcesCard {...baseProps} resources={buildResources({ containers: { main: c } })} />,
+      )
+      // Verify ALL gauge <text> nodes show "—" — not just the first — so the
+      // assertion survives future reordering of CPU/Memory/Disk gauges.
+      const texts = Array.from(container.querySelectorAll('text'))
+      expect(texts.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2) // at least CPU + Memory
+      for (const t of texts) expect(t.textContent).toBe('—')
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── LogsViewer ──
+  describe('LogsViewer', () => {
+    const baseProps = {
+      logs: null as string | null,
+      loading: false,
+      onRefresh: vi.fn(),
+    }
+
+    it('loading + no logs → shows loading inside the log region', () => {
+      const { getAllByText } = render(<LogsViewer {...baseProps} loading />)
+      expect(getAllByText('common.loading').length).toBeGreaterThan(0)
+    })
+
+    it('no logs + not loading → noLogsAvailable', () => {
+      const { getByText } = render(<LogsViewer {...baseProps} />)
+      expect(getByText('settings.noLogsAvailable')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('logs present → renders them in a <pre>', () => {
+      const { container } = render(<LogsViewer {...baseProps} logs="line1\nline2\n" />)
+      const pre = container.querySelector('pre')
+      expect(pre).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(pre?.textContent).toContain('line1')
+    })
+
+    it('Tail select default=100; change → setter + next Refresh carries new tail', () => {
+      const onRefresh = vi.fn()
+      const { container, getByText } = render(<LogsViewer {...baseProps} onRefresh={onRefresh} />)
+      const tailSelect = container.querySelector('select') as HTMLSelectElement
+      expect(tailSelect.value).toBe('100')
+      fireEvent.change(tailSelect, { target: { value: '500' } })
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('common.refresh'))
+      expect(onRefresh).toHaveBeenCalledWith({ tail: 500, timestamps: false })
+    })
+
+    it('Timestamps checkbox toggle → Refresh carries timestamps=true', () => {
+      const onRefresh = vi.fn()
+      const { container, getByText } = render(<LogsViewer {...baseProps} onRefresh={onRefresh} />)
+      const checkbox = container.querySelector('input[type="checkbox"]') as HTMLInputElement
+      fireEvent.click(checkbox)
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('common.refresh'))
+      expect(onRefresh).toHaveBeenCalledWith({ tail: 100, timestamps: true })
+    })
+
+    it('loading=true → Refresh button disabled', () => {
+      const { container, getAllByText } = render(<LogsViewer {...baseProps} loading />)
+      // Two "common.loading" texts may render (pre + button) — pick the button
+      const loadingButton = getAllByText('common.loading').find((el) => el.tagName === 'BUTTON') as HTMLButtonElement
+      expect(loadingButton).toBeDisabled()
+      // Sanity: checkbox and select stay enabled
+      expect(container.querySelector('select')).not.toBeDisabled()
+    })
+  })
+})

test(web): DiaryCards 全面覆盖 (#894 Step 5 补) (#1121)

## Summary

Epic #894 Step 5 (#899) — 再补一个：\`DiaryCards.tsx\` (150 LOC) 从 0% →
全分支，19 tests。

## 新增 19 个测试

| 组 | # | 覆盖 |
|---|---|---|
| 加载状态 | 1 | 3 个 animate-pulse skeleton |
| 空状态 | 3 | success=true+空文件 / success=false / API reject（finally 清
loading） |
| 列表渲染 | 4 | sort desc + slice(0,10) / first_user_message 兜底 session_id
/ archive_reason 兜底 "Archived session" / 卡片点击 →
router.push('/session-history') |
| formatDate | 6 | ISO 当天 "Today" / 其他日 weekday / Unix seconds / Unix ms
/ invalid → 原样 / 空串 → "Unknown" |
| 滚动控件 | 4 | files ≤ 3 隐藏箭头 / > 3 显示 / 点左 scrollBy(-280) / 点右
scrollBy(+280) |
| Unmount 竞态 | 1 | cancelled flag 防卸载后 setState |

## Harness 要点

- `vi.useFakeTimers()` 先于 `vi.setSystemTime()` — 吃过 #1119 round 2
的教训，`formatDate` 的 "Today" 判断依赖确定"今天"
- i18n mock 对 `t(key) || 'English'` 模式的 key 返回 `''` 触发英文兜底
- jsdom 不实现 `HTMLElement.scrollBy`，用 `Object.defineProperty` 手搭（同
ScrollToBottomButton）

## Bug-hunting

无新发现。组件 unmount race 有正确的 cancelled flag；三个 fallback 链都有。

## Test plan

- [x] 19/19 通过
- [x] tsc clean
- [x] lint clean
- [ ] CI 绿

## 关联

- Epic #894 Step 5 (#899)
- Step 5 已覆盖：ChannelsSection / Slack/Telegram/Discord/FeishuSetup
wizards / Integrations / ModelSection / TimezoneSection / SaveButton /
useClawSettings / useIntegrations / UsageCard (#1119) / 本 PR
- 剩余：ConnectorsSection (493 LOC) / DiagnosticsSection (338 LOC) /
ClawSettingsClient (438 LOC key ~120)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

files.patch: @@ -108,6 +108,7 @@ export default function DiaryCards() {

@@ -0,0 +1,287 @@
+// @vitest-environment jsdom
+import { act, cleanup, fireEvent, render } from '@testing-library/react'
+import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest'
+
+import type { ArchivedSessionFile } from '@/lib/api/session'
+
+// ── Hoisted mocks ──
+
+const hoisted = vi.hoisted(() => ({
+  useTranslation: vi.fn(),
+  useLocalizedRouter: vi.fn(),
+  getArchivedSessions: vi.fn(),
+  routerPush: vi.fn(),
+}))
+
+vi.mock('@/contexts/LanguageContext', () => ({
+  useTranslation: hoisted.useTranslation,
+}))
+vi.mock('@/hooks/useLocalizedRouter', () => ({
+  useLocalizedRouter: hoisted.useLocalizedRouter,
+}))
+vi.mock('@/lib/api/session', () => ({
+  getArchivedSessions: hoisted.getArchivedSessions,
+}))
+
+import DiaryCards from '@/app/[locale]/claw-settings/components/DiaryCards'
+
+// ── Fixtures ──
+
+function buildFile(overrides: Partial<ArchivedSessionFile> = {}): ArchivedSessionFile {
+  return {
+    file_path: `/archived/${overrides.session_id ?? 'sess1'}.json`,
+    session_id: 'sess1',
+    archive_timestamp: '2026-04-20T10:00:00Z',
+    archive_reason: 'inactive',
+    first_user_message: 'Hello world',
+    ...overrides,
+  } as ArchivedSessionFile
+}
+
+describe('DiaryCards', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    hoisted.useTranslation.mockReturnValue((key: string) => {
+      // Mirror source fallbacks: mock returns '' so `t(key) || 'Fallback'` picks the English.
+      if (key === 'settings.diary' || key === 'settings.diaryEmpty' || key === 'settings.diaryArchived') return ''
+      return key
+    })
+    hoisted.useLocalizedRouter.mockReturnValue({ push: hoisted.routerPush })
+    // Real timers by default — per web/CLAUDE.md, fake timers must not be
+    // mixed with findByText/waitFor. Only the formatDate describe below flips
+    // to fake timers, because it's the only group that needs setSystemTime.
+  })
+
+  afterEach(() => {
+    cleanup()
+    hoisted.routerPush.mockReset()
+    hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockReset()
+  })
+
+  // ── initial load states ──
+  describe('loading state', () => {
+    it('renders 3 animated skeleton placeholders while API is pending', () => {
+      // Return a never-resolving promise so the component stays in loading state.
+      hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockReturnValue(new Promise(() => {}))
+      const { container, getByText } = render(<DiaryCards />)
+      expect(getByText('Diary')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(container.querySelectorAll('.animate-pulse')).toHaveLength(3)
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe('empty state', () => {
+    it('no files returned → empty message, no skeleton, no cards', async () => {
+      hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockResolvedValue({ success: true, data: { files: [] } })
+      const { findByText, container } = render(<DiaryCards />)
+      expect(await findByText(/No sessions yet/)).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(container.querySelectorAll('.animate-pulse')).toHaveLength(0)
+      expect(container.querySelectorAll('button').length).toBe(0)
+    })
+
+    it('API response with success=false → stays at empty state (files never populated)', async () => {
+      hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockResolvedValue({ success: false, error: 'boom' })
+      const { findByText } = render(<DiaryCards />)
+      expect(await findByText(/No sessions yet/)).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('API rejects → loading clears via finally; component falls into empty state', async () => {
+      // Source uses `.then().finally()` without `.catch()` — rejection leaks
+      // as an unhandled promise rejection (tracked in #1127). Install a
+      // scoped `unhandledRejection` handler so vitest 4.x doesn't fail the
+      // file-level post-test validation. Once #1127 lands, drop the suppressor.
+      const unhandledHandler = () => {}
+      process.on('unhandledRejection', unhandledHandler)
+      try {
+        hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockRejectedValue(new Error('net'))
+        const { findByText } = render(<DiaryCards />)
+        expect(await findByText(/No sessions yet/)).toBeInTheDocument()
+      } finally {
+        process.removeListener('unhandledRejection', unhandledHandler)
+      }
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── list rendering + sorting + slicing ──
+  describe('list state', () => {
+    it('renders files sorted by archive_timestamp desc and sliced to 10', async () => {
+      // 12 files, reversed ISO timestamps so natural order doesn't match desired sort.
+      const files = Array.from({ length: 12 }, (_, i) =>
+        buildFile({
+          session_id: `s${i}`,
+          file_path: `/p/s${i}`,
+          archive_timestamp: `2026-04-${String(10 + i).padStart(2, '0')}T00:00:00Z`,
+          first_user_message: `msg-${i}`,
+        }),
+      )
+      hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockResolvedValue({ success: true, data: { files } })
+      const { findByText, queryByText } = render(<DiaryCards />)
+      // Newest (idx 11, timestamp ending in 21) renders
+      expect(await findByText('msg-11')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      // Oldest in returned slice = after sort desc + slice 0..10, msg-2 is the 10th
+      expect(await findByText('msg-2')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      // msg-0 / msg-1 are outside the 10-slice (too old)
+      expect(queryByText('msg-0')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(queryByText('msg-1')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('fallback: no first_user_message → session_id used as card title', async () => {
+      const files = [buildFile({ session_id: 'sess-only', first_user_message: '' })]
+      hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockResolvedValue({ success: true, data: { files } })
+      const { findByText } = render(<DiaryCards />)
+      expect(await findByText('sess-only')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('fallback: no archive_reason → falls back to Archived session default', async () => {
+      const files = [buildFile({ archive_reason: '' })]
+      hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockResolvedValue({ success: true, data: { files } })
+      const { findByText } = render(<DiaryCards />)
+      // i18n mock returns '' for settings.diaryArchived so the `|| 'Archived session'` English fallback wins.
+      expect(await findByText('Archived session')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('click any card → router.push("/session-history")', async () => {
+      const files = [buildFile({ first_user_message: 'open me' })]
+      hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockResolvedValue({ success: true, data: { files } })
+      const { findByText } = render(<DiaryCards />)
+      fireEvent.click((await findByText('open me')).closest('button') as HTMLElement)
+      expect(hoisted.routerPush).toHaveBeenCalledWith('/session-history')
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── formatDate branches ──
+  describe('formatDate', () => {
+    // Only this suite needs a stable "today" — scope the fake-timer setup
+    // here so the other describes above don't fight findByText/waitFor.
+    beforeEach(() => {
+      vi.useFakeTimers({ shouldAdvanceTime: true })
+      vi.setSystemTime(new Date('2026-04-20T12:00:00Z'))
+    })
+    afterEach(() => {
+      vi.useRealTimers()
+    })
+
+    // Observed through the rendered card's timestamp label.
+    async function renderWithTimestamp(ts: string) {
+      hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockResolvedValue({
+        success: true,
+        data: { files: [buildFile({ archive_timestamp: ts, first_user_message: 'x' })] },
+      })
+      const view = render(<DiaryCards />)
+      await view.findByText('x')
+      return view.container.querySelector('button')!
+    }
+
+    it('ISO string matching current system date → appends "· Today"', async () => {
+      const card = await renderWithTimestamp('2026-04-20T08:00:00Z')
+      expect(card.textContent).toContain('Today')
+    })
+
+    it('ISO string from a different day → appends weekday name', async () => {
+      const card = await renderWithTimestamp('2026-04-17T08:00:00Z') // Friday
+      expect(card.textContent).not.toContain('Today')
+      expect(card.textContent).toMatch(/Apr 17 · (Mon|Tue|Wed|Thu|Fri|Sat|Sun)/)
+    })
+
+    it('Unix seconds (< 1e12) → parsed × 1000 correctly', async () => {
+      const seconds = Math.floor(Date.UTC(2026, 3, 15, 9, 0, 0) / 1000)
+      const card = await renderWithTimestamp(String(seconds))
+      expect(card.textContent).toMatch(/Apr 15/)
+    })
+
+    it('Unix milliseconds (>= 1e12) → parsed directly', async () => {
+      const ms = Date.UTC(2026, 3, 12, 9, 0, 0)
+      const card = await renderWithTimestamp(String(ms))
+      expect(card.textContent).toMatch(/Apr 12/)
+    })
+
+    it('invalid date string → falls back to the original timestamp', async () => {
+      const card = await renderWithTimestamp('not-a-date')
+      expect(card.textContent).toContain('not-a-date')
+    })
+
+    it('empty string → falls back to "Unknown"', async () => {
+      const card = await renderWithTimestamp('')
+      expect(card.textContent).toContain('Unknown')
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── scroll controls ──
+  describe('scroll controls visibility + behavior', () => {
+    it('files.length <= 3 → scroll buttons hidden', async () => {
+      const files = Array.from({ length: 3 }, (_, i) => buildFile({ session_id: `s${i}`, file_path: `/p/s${i}` }))
+      hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockResolvedValue({ success: true, data: { files } })
+      const { findAllByRole, container } = render(<DiaryCards />)
+      await findAllByRole('button')
+      // The 3 card buttons render; 0 scroll buttons (arrows) — total = 3.
+      expect(container.querySelectorAll('button')).toHaveLength(3)
+    })
+
+    it('files.length > 3 → left/right scroll buttons render + pagination dots', async () => {
+      const files = Array.from({ length: 5 }, (_, i) =>
+        buildFile({
+          session_id: `s${i}`,
+          file_path: `/p/s${i}`,
+          archive_timestamp: `2026-04-${String(10 + i).padStart(2, '0')}T00:00:00Z`,
+        }),
+      )
+      hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockResolvedValue({ success: true, data: { files } })
+      const { findAllByRole, container } = render(<DiaryCards />)
+      await findAllByRole('button')
+      // 5 cards + 2 arrow buttons = 7 total
+      expect(container.querySelectorAll('button')).toHaveLength(7)
+    })
+
+    it('click left arrow → scrollBy(-280, smooth)', async () => {
+      const files = Array.from({ length: 5 }, (_, i) => buildFile({ session_id: `s${i}`, file_path: `/p/s${i}` }))
+      hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockResolvedValue({ success: true, data: { files } })
+      const { findByRole, container } = render(<DiaryCards />)
+      const leftArrow = await findByRole('button', { name: /scroll diary left/i })
+
+      // jsdom doesn't implement scrollBy on HTMLElement; install a spy via defineProperty.
+      const scrollRegion = container.querySelector('.scrollbar-hide') as HTMLElement
+      const scrollBy = vi.fn()
+      Object.defineProperty(scrollRegion, 'scrollBy', { configurable: true, value: scrollBy })
+      fireEvent.click(leftArrow)
+      expect(scrollBy).toHaveBeenCalledWith({ left: -280, behavior: 'smooth' })
+    })
+
+    it('click right arrow → scrollBy(+280, smooth)', async () => {
+      const files = Array.from({ length: 5 }, (_, i) => buildFile({ session_id: `s${i}`, file_path: `/p/s${i}` }))
+      hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockResolvedValue({ success: true, data: { files } })
+      const { findByRole, container } = render(<DiaryCards />)
+      const rightArrow = await findByRole('button', { name: /scroll diary right/i })
+
+      const scrollRegion = container.querySelector('.scrollbar-hide') as HTMLElement
+      const scrollBy = vi.fn()
+      Object.defineProperty(scrollRegion, 'scrollBy', { configurable: true, value: scrollBy })
+      fireEvent.click(rightArrow)
+      expect(scrollBy).toHaveBeenCalledWith({ left: 280, behavior: 'smooth' })
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── unmount race ──
+  describe('unmount race (cancelled flag)', () => {
+    it('unmount before API resolves → no setState warnings after resolve', async () => {
+      let resolveApi: (v: unknown) => void = () => {}
+      hoisted.getArchivedSessions.mockReturnValue(
+        new Promise((resolve) => {
+          resolveApi = resolve
+        }),
+      )
+      const { unmount } = render(<DiaryCards />)
+      unmount()
+      // Track any React act()/setState-on-unmounted warnings.
+      const consoleError = vi.spyOn(console, 'error').mockImplementation(() => {})
+      // Resolve AFTER unmount — the cancelled flag must prevent setState.
+      await act(async () => {
+        resolveApi({ success: true, data: { files: [buildFile()] } })
+        // Yield to the macrotask queue so the Promise's .then/.finally
+        // microtask chain drains fully before we inspect console.error.
+        await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 0))
+      })
+      // If the cancelled flag is broken, React will log a setState-on-unmounted
+      // warning. No warning ⇒ the guard held.
+      expect(consoleError).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
+      consoleError.mockRestore()
+    })
+  })
+})

commit.message:
feat(web): replace degraded banner with IQ bar (#1123)

## Summary
- Replace the text-only "积分已用完，正在使用基础模型运行中" degraded banner with a
visual **intelligence bar** (gradient red→green, indicator at 25/100)
- New messaging: "AI 变笨了" / "AI is underpowered" — users immediately
understand quality trade-off instead of being confused by "basic model"
wording
- Theme-aware: IQ bar gradient colors defined as CSS vars
(`ecap-iq-bar-*`) with dark mode support
- 3 new i18n keys (`degradedTitle`, `degradedScore`, `boostAI`) across
all 7 languages

## Motivation
Users on free models attributed poor AI output quality to product bugs
rather than model capability. This redesign makes the quality difference
unmistakably clear.

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify degraded banner renders IQ bar in light mode
- [ ] Verify degraded banner renders correctly in dark mode
- [ ] Verify `expired` and `expiryWarning` variants are unchanged
- [ ] Check all 7 languages display correctly (zh, en, ja, ko, es, pt,
ar)
- [ ] Click "充值提升" / "Boost AI" navigates to `/subscription`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- [ ] Verify degraded banner renders correctly in dark mode
- [ ] Verify `expired` and `expiryWarning` variants are unchanged
- [ ] Check all 7 languages display correctly (zh, en, ja, ko, es, pt,
ar)
- [ ] Click "充值提升" / "Boost AI" navigates to `/subscription`

@@ -0,0 +1,68 @@
+# Degraded Banner — IQ Bar Redesign
+
+## Problem
+
+When users exhaust their credits, the system falls back to free/basic models. The current banner says "积分已用完，正在使用基础模型运行中" — users don't understand what "basic model" means and attribute the resulting bugs/poor quality to the product itself, not to model capability. This causes support confusion and churn.
+
+## Goal
+
+Replace the `degraded` variant of `ModelDegradationBanner` with a visual "intelligence bar" that intuitively communicates: **your AI is currently dumb, pay to make it smart**.
+
+## Scope
+
+- **In scope:** `degraded` variant only (credits exhausted, using free model)
+- **Out of scope:** `expired` and `expiryWarning` variants remain unchanged
+
+## Design
+
+### Layout (top banner, ~36px height)
+
+```
+[🧠] [AI 变笨了 · 智力 25/100] [====gradient bar====] [充值提升]
+```
+
+Components left-to-right:
+1. **Brain emoji** (🧠) — visual anchor
+2. **Text block:**
+   - Title: "AI 变笨了" (bold, warning color)
+   - Separator dot
+   - Score: "智力 25/100" (muted)
+3. **Gradient progress bar** (6px height):
+   - Red→Yellow→Green gradient background (0%→40%→75%→100%)
+   - White circle indicator at 25% position (current intelligence level)
+   - Rounded corners
+4. **CTA button**: "充值提升" / "Boost AI"
+
+### Visual Specifications
+
+- Background: `bg-ecap-warning-bg` (existing token, warm yellow)
+- Border: `border-ecap-warning-border` bottom border
+- Bar gradient: `#ef4444` → `#f59e0b` → `#22c55e` → `#10b981`
+- Indicator: 10px white circle, 2px border matching `ecap-warning-text`, drop shadow
+- Button: solid `#d4940a` background, white text
+- Intelligence score: hardcoded 25 (free model) — not dynamically calculated
+
+### i18n Keys
+
+Replace `modelDegradation.banner` and `modelDegradation.addCredits` with new keys for the `degraded` variant:
+
+| Key | zh | en | ja | ko | es | pt | ar |
+|-----|----|----|----|----|----|----|-----|
+| `modelDegradation.degradedTitle` | AI 变笨了 | AI is underpowered | AIの知能が低下中 | AI 성능 저하 중 | IA con bajo rendimiento | IA com baixo desempenho | الذكاء الاصطناعي ضعيف الأداء |
+| `modelDegradation.degradedScore` | 智力 {score}/100 | IQ {score}/100 | 知能 {score}/100 | 지능 {score}/100 | IQ {score}/100 | QI {score}/100 | الذكاء {score}/100 |
+| `modelDegradation.boostAI` | 充值提升 | Boost AI | 知能UP | AI 부스트 | Potenciar IA | Turbinar IA | تعزيز الذكاء |
+
+Existing keys (`banner`, `addCredits`) are kept for backward safety but no longer used by the `degraded` variant.
+
+## Files to Modify
+
+1. **`web/src/app/[locale]/chat/components/ModelDegradationBanner.tsx`** — redesign `degraded` variant rendering
+2. **`web/src/locales/{zh,en,ja,ko,es,pt,ar}.ts`** — add 3 new i18n keys each
+3. **`web/tests/e2e/specs/insufficient-credits.spec.ts`** — update if it asserts on banner text/structure
+
+## Non-Goals
+
+- No changes to `useBillingCredits` hook or API layer
+- No dynamic intelligence score calculation — hardcode 25 for free model
+- No animation or transitions on the progress bar
+- No changes to `expired` or `expiryWarning` variants

+// @vitest-environment jsdom
+import { cleanup, fireEvent, render, screen } from '@testing-library/react'
+import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest'
+
+// ── Hoisted mocks ──
+const hoisted = vi.hoisted(() => ({
+  useTranslation: vi.fn(),
+  useBillingCredits: vi.fn(),
+  useRouter: vi.fn(),
+}))
+
+vi.mock('@/contexts/LanguageContext', () => ({
+  useTranslation: hoisted.useTranslation,
+}))
+vi.mock('@/hooks/useBillingCredits', () => ({
+  useBillingCredits: hoisted.useBillingCredits,
+}))
+vi.mock('@/hooks/useLocalizedRouter', () => ({
+  useLocalizedRouter: hoisted.useRouter,
+}))
+
+// Lazy import to ensure mocks are in place
+const loadComponent = () => import('@/app/[locale]/chat/components/ModelDegradationBanner')
+
+function defaultCredits(overrides: Record<string, unknown> = {}) {
+  return {
+    degraded: false,
+    noSubscription: false,
+    hasFetched: true,
+    subscriptionStatus: 'active',
+    currentPeriodEnd: null,
+    ...overrides,
+  }
+}
+
+describe('ModelDegradationBanner', () => {
+  const pushMock = vi.fn()
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    hoisted.useTranslation.mockReturnValue((key: string) => key)
+    hoisted.useRouter.mockReturnValue({ push: pushMock })
+    hoisted.useBillingCredits.mockReturnValue(defaultCredits())
+  })
+
+  afterEach(() => {
+    cleanup()
+    vi.clearAllMocks()
+  })
+
+  it('renders nothing when hasFetched is false', async () => {
+    hoisted.useBillingCredits.mockReturnValue(defaultCredits({ hasFetched: false }))
+    const { default: Banner } = await loadComponent()
+    const { container } = render(<Banner />)
+    expect(container.firstChild).toBeNull()
+  })
+
+  it('renders nothing when no degradation/subscription issues', async () => {
+    const { default: Banner } = await loadComponent()
+    const { container } = render(<Banner />)
+    expect(container.firstChild).toBeNull()
+  })
+
+  // ── Degraded variant (IQ bar) ──
+
+  describe('degraded variant', () => {
+    beforeEach(() => {
+      hoisted.useBillingCredits.mockReturnValue(defaultCredits({ degraded: true }))
+    })
+
+    it('renders IQ bar with brain emoji and score', async () => {
+      const { default: Banner } = await loadComponent()
+      render(<Banner />)
+
+      const banner = screen.getByTestId('genclaw-degradation-banner')
+      expect(banner).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(banner.dataset.variant).toBe('degraded')
+      expect(banner).toHaveTextContent('🧠')
+      expect(banner).toHaveTextContent('modelDegradation.degradedTitle')
+      expect(banner).toHaveTextContent('modelDegradation.degradedScore')
+    })
+
+    it('replaces {score} placeholder with 25', async () => {
+      hoisted.useTranslation.mockReturnValue((key: string) => {
+        if (key === 'modelDegradation.degradedScore') return 'IQ {score}/100'
+        return key
+      })
+      const { default: Banner } = await loadComponent()
+      render(<Banner />)
+
+      expect(screen.getByTestId('genclaw-degradation-banner')).toHaveTextContent('IQ 25/100')
+    })
+
+    it('renders boost button that navigates to /subscription', async () => {
+      const { default: Banner } = await loadComponent()
+      render(<Banner />)
+
+      const button = screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'modelDegradation.boostAI' })
+      expect(button).toBeInTheDocument()
+      fireEvent.click(button)
+      expect(pushMock).toHaveBeenCalledWith('/subscription')
+    })
+
+    it('renders gradient progress bar with indicator', async () => {
+      const { default: Banner } = await loadComponent()
+      render(<Banner />)
+
+      const banner = screen.getByTestId('genclaw-degradation-banner')
+      // Gradient bar container
+      const gradientBar = banner.querySelector('[class*="rounded-full"][class*="relative"]')
+      expect(gradientBar).toBeInTheDocument()
+      // Indicator dot
+      const indicator = gradientBar?.querySelector('[class*="rounded-full"][class*="border-2"]')
+      expect(indicator).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── Expired variant ──
+
+  describe('expired variant', () => {
+    beforeEach(() => {
+      hoisted.useBillingCredits.mockReturnValue(defaultCredits({ noSubscription: true }))
+    })
+
+    it('renders expired message with subscribe button', async () => {
+      const { default: Banner } = await loadComponent()
+      render(<Banner />)
+
+      const banner = screen.getByTestId('genclaw-degradation-banner')
+      expect(banner.dataset.variant).toBe('expired')
+      expect(banner).toHaveTextContent('modelDegradation.expired')
+
+      const button = screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'modelDegradation.subscribe' })
+      fireEvent.click(button)
+      expect(pushMock).toHaveBeenCalledWith('/subscription')
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── ExpiryWarning variant ──
+
+  describe('expiryWarning variant', () => {
+    it('renders expiry warning with days remaining', async () => {
+      const nowSec = Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)
+      hoisted.useBillingCredits.mockReturnValue(
+        defaultCredits({
+          subscriptionStatus: 'trial',
+          currentPeriodEnd: nowSec + 2 * 86400,
+        }),
+      )
+      hoisted.useTranslation.mockReturnValue((key: string) => {
+        if (key === 'modelDegradation.expiryWarning') return 'Expires in {days} day(s)'
+        return key
+      })
+
+      const { default: Banner } = await loadComponent()
+      render(<Banner />)
+
+      const banner = screen.getByTestId('genclaw-degradation-banner')
+      expect(banner.dataset.variant).toBe('expiryWarning')
+      expect(banner).toHaveTextContent('Expires in 2 day(s)')
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── Priority ──
+
+  describe('priority: expired > expiryWarning > degraded', () => {
+    it('shows expired when both noSubscription and degraded', async () => {
+      hoisted.useBillingCredits.mockReturnValue(defaultCredits({ noSubscription: true, degraded: true }))
+      const { default: Banner } = await loadComponent()
+      render(<Banner />)
+
+      expect(screen.getByTestId('genclaw-degradation-banner').dataset.variant).toBe('expired')
+    })
+
+    it('shows expiryWarning over degraded when in expiry window', async () => {
+      const nowSec = Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)
+      hoisted.useBillingCredits.mockReturnValue(
+        defaultCredits({
+          degraded: true,
+          subscriptionStatus: 'canceling',
+          currentPeriodEnd: nowSec + 86400,
+        }),
+      )
+      const { default: Banner } = await loadComponent()
+      render(<Banner />)
+
+      expect(screen.getByTestId('genclaw-degradation-banner').dataset.variant).toBe('expiryWarning')
+    })
+  })
+})

commit.message:
feat(web): preview Mattermost attachments + pptx rendering fixes (#1125)

## Summary

- **Preview MM attachments inline**: clicking a previewable file
attachment (pdf / xlsx / docx / pptx / html / md / code / ...) on a
Mattermost message now opens the artifact preview side panel, and
bot-sent attachments auto-open like today's in-text artifact URLs.
- **Infrastructure**: adds an optional `PreviewSource` on `PreviewFile`
carrying the MM Bearer token. A new `useResolvedUrl` hook fetches MM
URLs with auth once per preview, caches them as blob URLs, and cleans up
on unmount — renderers stay auth-unaware.
- **pptx renderer overhaul**: the existing hand-rolled OOXML renderer
had a long tail of bugs exposed by real-world decks. This PR fixes each
one with a principled spec-based solution.

## What changed in pptx rendering

Text:
- Strip BiDi control chars (stray `U+202E` was reversing titles)
- Drop `display: flex` on paragraphs → flex items were trimming
leading/trailing whitespace, dropping spaces between
differently-formatted runs
- `overflow: visible` on text shapes — PowerPoint lets text overflow
shape bounds; we were clipping tight table cells
- Honor `<a:ea>` / `<a:latin>` typeface with CJK-aware fallback stack
(YaHei → PingFang → Noto CJK)
- Font size in `cqw` units against a per-slide CSS variable → text
scales with the slide container (same behavior as PowerPoint canvas
resize)
- Parse `<a:spcBef>`, `<a:spcAft>`, `<a:lnSpc>` with sensible default
(0.4em) when master defaults would have applied
- Render `<a:buChar>` bullets with hanging indent from `marL` + `indent`

Shapes:
- Parse `<a:custGeom>` paths (moveTo / lnTo / cubicBezTo / quadBezTo /
arcTo / close) to SVG
- Multi-subpath support (was only reading first `<a:path>` of
`<a:pathLst>`)
- `prstGeom` presets as SVG paths: ellipse, line, triangle, diamond,
parallelogram, hexagon, arrows, star5, arc, blockArc
- `prst="arc"` sweep angles from `<a:avLst>` adj1/adj2 with correct
normalization (`?: sw1 sw1 sw2`)
- `xfrm` `rot` / `flipH` / `flipV` applied as CSS transforms
- `roundRect` corner radius uses spec formula `adj / 100000 × min(w, h)
/ 2`
- Border `<a:alpha>` now applied (was ignored — borders rendered at 100%
opacity)

Layout:
- Drop the `max-w-3xl` cap on the preview container so slides fill the
preview pane

Misc:
- Force correct MIME on MM blob URLs (MM serves
`application/octet-stream`, which made `<iframe>` show HTML source text)
- Hide "copy link" on MM-sourced previews (blob URLs aren't shareable)

## Files

- New: `web/src/components/artifacts/useResolvedUrl.ts`,
`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-mm-attachment-preview.md`
- Modified: `ArtifactPreview.tsx`, `types.ts`, `MMAttachments.tsx`,
`useArtifactsSidebar.ts`, `MarkdownContent.tsx`, `GenClawClient.tsx`,
`PptxRenderer.tsx`

## Test plan

- [ ] Send pdf / xlsx / docx / pptx / html / md / code / csv as MM
attachments → each opens in preview on click; bot-sent attachments
auto-open
- [ ] Download button downloads original file (not blob) with correct
extension
- [ ] Copy-link hidden for MM previews, visible for artifact URLs
- [ ] Existing artifact URL previews unchanged (regression check on
public URLs)
- [ ] pptx: text renders correctly (no reversed titles, no missing
spaces, Chinese fonts OK)
- [ ] pptx: curves / arcs / non-rect shapes render (not collapsed to
rectangles)
- [ ] pptx: preview fills full preview pane width; fonts scale
proportionally

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,104 @@
+# Mattermost Attachment Preview Integration
+
+## Summary
+
+Make Mattermost file attachments (sent via the message tool) open in the existing artifact preview UI when their extension matches `PREVIEWABLE_EXTS`. Mattermost files require Bearer-token auth, so preview infrastructure needs to carry an optional auth source through to the renderers.
+
+## Scope
+
+**In scope**: Non-image/audio/video MM file attachments whose extension is in `PREVIEWABLE_EXTS` (pdf, html, csv, md, xlsx, docx, pptx, code files, etc.). Exact same type set as current artifact preview — no additions, no subtractions.
+
+**Out of scope**:
+- Image attachments — already handled by `window.openImagePreview` (separate system, leave alone)
+- Audio/video attachments — rendered via `<audio>`/`<video>` with blob URLs (leave alone)
+- Markdown text that *links to* MM files — only direct attachments
+
+## Requirements
+
+1. Clicking a previewable MM `FileAttachment` card opens the artifact preview side panel
+2. Preview works for all existing previewable types, including iframe-based renderers (pdf, html)
+3. Download button in preview fetches with auth, triggers download — no broken 401 link
+4. Copy-link button is hidden for MM files (blob URLs are non-shareable)
+5. Reload button re-fetches with auth (not just URL cache-bust)
+6. No regression for existing public artifact previews
+
+## Architecture
+
+### Data model
+
+```ts
+type PreviewSource =
+  | { kind: 'public' }                      // default — today's behavior
+  | { kind: 'mattermost'; token: string }   // Bearer-token auth
+
+interface PreviewFile {
+  name: string
+  url: string       // canonical URL (display, download semantics, reload key)
+  ext: string
+  source?: PreviewSource  // omitted = public
+}
+```
+
+Why a discriminated union (not a `headers` field): the download/copy-link semantics branch on source *kind*, not on raw headers. The union keeps that branching type-safe and extensible.
+
+### Two fetch paths, one resolved URL
+
+All existing renderers consume a single `url: string` prop and internally `fetch()` or embed `<iframe src>`. None supports custom headers. Rather than refactor every renderer to plumb auth, resolve the URL *once* at the preview shell and hand every renderer a URL they can fetch without auth:
+
+- **public**: resolved URL = original URL (no change)
+- **mattermost**: auth-fetch → blob → `URL.createObjectURL` → blob URL. Renderers fetch/embed the blob URL; iframes work because blob URLs are same-origin-like
+
+A new hook `useResolvedUrl(file)` encapsulates this, including `URL.revokeObjectURL` cleanup.
+
+### Reload semantics
+
+- **public**: bump cache-buster query param (current behavior)
+- **mattermost**: trigger `useResolvedUrl.reload()` → re-fetch with auth, new blob URL
+
+### Download button
+
+- **public**: `fetch(url, { cache: 'no-store' })` → blob → anchor download (current)
+- **mattermost**: `fetch(url, { headers: { Authorization: Bearer … } })` → blob → anchor download
+
+### Copy-link button
+
+Hidden when `source.kind !== 'public'` — blob URLs aren't shareable and MM URLs require the token.
+
+### FileAvailabilityGate
+
+Purpose: artifact files are written async after URL is assigned; gate retries until HTTP 200. For MM, the auth-fetch in `useResolvedUrl` already serves as availability check, so the gate is skipped for MM files.
+
+## File changes
+
+### PR 1 — preview infrastructure (no MM integration yet)
+
+| File | Change |
+|---|---|
+| `web/src/components/artifacts/types.ts` | Add `PreviewSource`, extend `PreviewFile` with optional `source` |
+| `web/src/components/artifacts/useResolvedUrl.ts` **(new)** | Hook: public → passthrough; mattermost → auth-fetch → blob URL + cleanup |
+| `web/src/components/artifacts/ArtifactPreview.tsx` | Consume `useResolvedUrl`; branch download/copy/reload/gate on source |
+| `web/src/app/[locale]/chat/hooks/useArtifactsSidebar.ts` | `window.openFilePreview` signature grows optional `source` param |
+| `web/src/components/MarkdownContent.tsx` | Update `Window.openFilePreview` type decl (back-compat — arg is optional) |
+
+Acceptance: all current artifact previews behave identically (source omitted = public passthrough).
+
+### PR 2 — MM FileAttachment integration
+
+| File | Change |
+|---|---|
+| `web/src/components/mattermost/MMAttachments.tsx` | `FileAttachment`: derive ext from `file.name`; if `PREVIEWABLE_EXTS.includes(ext)` make the card clickable, dispatch `window.openFilePreview(fileURL, name, ext, { kind: 'mattermost', token })` |
+
+Acceptance: upload each previewable type via MM and verify preview opens, renders, downloads, reloads.
+
+## Non-goals / deferred
+
+- Image preview unification — `openImagePreview` stays separate
+- Signed URL or backend proxy for MM files — blob URL approach is sufficient
+- Mattermost permalink "copy link" — requires post ID plumbing; drop the button for now
+- Extending `PREVIEWABLE_EXTS` — keep parity with existing list
+
+## Risks
+
+1. **HTML iframe + blob URL + sandbox**: blob URLs under sandbox iframe may hit CSP quirks. Test manually; if broken, downgrade html to source-only view for MM files (fallback path already exists via `SourceRenderer`).
+2. **Large files in memory**: blob holds full file in browser memory. For pptx/xlsx >50MB the browser may OOM. Acceptable for current use cases; revisit if reports come in.
+3. **Token lifetime**: the token is captured at `openFilePreview` dispatch time. If it expires mid-session and the user clicks reload, the re-fetch 401s. Error path shows "failed to load" — acceptable; user re-opens the attachment to capture a fresh token.

commit.message:
refactor(web): extract triggerCreditsRefresh to src/lib (A1-PR3) (#1124)

## Summary
- First A1 cleanup PR — fixes 1 of the 2 W1 violations in the baseline.
- Moves the plain-function `triggerCreditsRefresh` out of
`src/hooks/useBillingCredits.ts` into a new
`src/lib/billing-credits.ts`.
- Rewrites **all 3** call sites (no re-export shim) and shrinks the
baseline 18 → 17.

## Why `triggerCreditsRefresh` doesn't belong in `hooks/`
```ts
export function triggerCreditsRefresh(): void {
  if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
    window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('credits-refresh'))
  }
}
```
No React state, no `use*` dependency — it's a plain event dispatcher
that happened to live alongside the `useBillingCredits` hook. Any `lib/`
module needing to signal "refresh credits" had to import from
`@/hooks/*`, which is exactly the W1 contract violation (`lib → hooks` —
upward dependency).

## Changes

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `web/src/lib/billing-credits.ts` | **new** — 14 lines, single function
|
| `web/src/hooks/useBillingCredits.ts` | drop the function + its
comment; `getCachedCredits` / `clearCreditsCache` / hook stay |
| `src/components/billing/SubscriptionPanel.tsx` | `@/hooks` →
`@/lib/billing-credits` for this symbol |
| `src/components/onboarding/OnboardingProvider.tsx` | same |
| `src/lib/payment/handlePaymentSuccess.ts` | same (the W1 violator) |
| `tests/unit/lib/billing-credits.unit.spec.ts` | **new** — 2 tests
moved from hooks spec |
| `tests/unit/hooks/useBillingCredits.unit.spec.ts` | describe block
removed + import trimmed |
| `tests/unit/lib/payment/handlePaymentSuccess.unit.spec.ts` | mock path
+ dynamic-import path |
| `tests/unit/components/billing/SubscriptionPanel.unit.spec.tsx` |
split single `vi.mock(@/hooks/useBillingCredits)` into 2 mocks |
| `web/.dependency-cruiser-known-violations.json` | removed the fixed
`handlePaymentSuccess.ts → useBillingCredits.ts` entry |

No re-export shim is left behind, per the project convention
(`feedback_no_reexport_shim` in the author's memory).

## Baseline shrinks 18 → 17
The `depcruise-baseline` pattern designed in A1-PR2 works as expected:
each cleanup PR removes one entry from the baseline JSON and fixes the
corresponding import. When the JSON is `[]` the baseline file itself can
be deleted.

Remaining W1: `src/lib/seo.ts → @/theme/brand-assets` (11-caller
refactor, deferred to its own PR).

## Local verification
- `pnpm lint:imports` — exit 0, prints `17 known violations ignored`
- `pnpm test:unit` on the 4 affected files — **68 tests pass**
- `pnpm lint` — clean (simple-import-sort auto-fixed during commit)
- `npx tsc --noEmit` — clean

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests pass (all 4 affected files)
- [x] dep-cruiser hard gate still green (baseline shrinks 18 → 17)
- [ ] CI confirms above
- [ ] Reviewer validates split mock approach in
`SubscriptionPanel.unit.spec.tsx` (one `vi.mock` per module)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message: test(e2e): add model switching, onboarding & subscription E2E tests (#1019) ## Summary - **Model Switching**: selector button, dropdown tabs, tab filtering, model selection, ESC close - **Onboarding Flow**: full 4-step UI via `?onboarding=preview` mode (invite code → name → companion → loading) - **Subscription & Pricing**: public pricing page, subscription panel, billing toggle, plan CTAs; staging-only Stripe sandbox tests (upgrade redirect, downgrade/cancel modals) ## Files - `web/tests/e2e/specs/model-switching.spec.ts` — 5 scenarios - `web/tests/e2e/specs/onboarding-flow.spec.ts` — 5 scenarios - `web/tests/e2e/specs/subscription-pricing.spec.ts` — 10 scenarios (3 staging-only) - `web/playwright.config.ts` — register 3 new projects ## Test plan - [ ] `pnpm exec playwright test --project=model-switching` - [ ] `pnpm exec playwright test --project=onboarding-flow` - [ ] `pnpm exec playwright test --project=subscription-pricing` - [ ] Staging: `E2E_ENV=staging pnpm exec playwright test --project=subscription-pricing` ## Follow-up fixes ### `onboarding-flow` — preview-mode starts on step 1 again Local run against staging surfaced one real bug: **Symptom**: Scenario 1 "Step 1 — Invite Code entry" timed out with Continue button `[disabled]`; 3 downstream scenarios skipped via serial mode. **Root cause**: two conflicts with the shared auth state / page chrome: 1. `auth.setup.ts` seeds `ecap:onboarding:progress` marking every step completed (so other specs bypass the modal). This spec inherited that storage state and was landing on Companion Select instead of step 1. 2. `GuideTourModal` (`fixed inset-0 z-[100]`) overlays the viewport on fresh sessions and intercepts pointer events on the invite code form — Playwright log: ` subtree intercepts pointer events`. **Fix** (`onboarding-flow.spec.ts` `beforeAll`, no business-code changes): - Strip `ecap:onboarding:progress*` keys via `addInitScript` so React reads `DEFAULT_PROGRESS` and renders step 1. - Apply `E2E_OVERLAY_SUPPRESSION` (same set used by `model-switching` / `agent-hire-fire`) to dismiss `GuideTourModal` + compensation popup. - Scenario 1: wait for `"Got an invite code?"` prompt before interacting, switch to `input[placeholder="Enter code"]` + `pressSequentially('TESTCODE', { delay: 30 })` so the controlled input's `onChange` (with `.toUpperCase()` transform) reliably fires, and `await expect(continueBtn).toBeEnabled()` before click so a future state-sync regression surfaces in 5s rather than a 180s disabled-click timeout. **Local verification**: `E2E_ENV=staging pnpm test:e2e --project=onboarding-flow` → **6/6 passed in 21.5s**. ### Auto-review follow-ups - **Stripe popup race** (subscription-pricing Upgrade scenario): `popup.url()` was read the instant the event fired, but Stripe opens `about:blank` first and then redirects — capture could be `about:blank` and silently fail. Also, `page.on('popup', ...)` leaks a long-lived handler across tests. Switched to a scoped `page.waitForEvent('popup')` raced against the click, then `popup.waitForURL(/checkout\.stripe\.com/)` before reading the URL. - **Tailwind-class locator** (model-switching selection scenario): `option.locator('.text-sm.font-medium')` violated `web/CLAUDE.md`'s "Tailwind class strings are styling, not contract" rule. Added `data-testid="model-option-name"` on the model display-name div and updated the test to use `getByTestId`. - **Dead redirect fallback in upgrade assertion**: `SubscriptionPanel` always uses `window.open(..., '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer')` — there is no in-tab redirect path. The previous `else { expect(page.url()).toContain(...) }` was dead code that produced a confusing `/en/subscription does not contain checkout.stripe.com` message when the popup event was missed. Replaced with two explicit assertions: popup must be captured (self-explanatory failure message) + captured URL must contain `checkout.stripe.com`. ### Known non-blockers (not addressed in this PR) - `waitForTimeout(300)` after tab clicks in a few places — pragmatic transition-animation padding; would only be worth revisiting if slow CI produces flake. - Hardcoded `100`/`200` price assertions — already scoped to `plan-card-pro` / `plan-card-ultra` so no broad matching; easier to update when prices actually change. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context)

Also, `page.on('popup', ...)` leaks a long-lived handler across tests. +import { type Page } from '@playwright/test'
+import path from 'path'
+
+import { expect, test } from '../fixtures/base'
+import { AGENTS, E2E_OVERLAY_SUPPRESSION, TIMEOUTS } from '../fixtures/test-data'
+
+const AUTH_STATE_PATH = path.resolve(__dirname, '../.auth/user.json')
+
+/**
+ * E2E: Model Switching
+ *
+ * Tests the ModelSelector component on the fullstack_assistant agent chat page:
+ *   1. Model selector button visible with a default model name
+ *   2. Dropdown opens with text/image/video tabs
+ *   3. Tab switching filters models correctly
+ *   4. Selecting a different model updates the button label
+ *   5. Dropdown closes after selection
+ *   6. ESC key closes the dropdown
+ */
+test.describe('Feature: Model Switching', () => {
+  test.describe.configure({ mode: 'serial' })
+
+  let sharedPage: Page
+
+  test.beforeAll(async ({ browser }) => {
+    const context = await browser.newContext({ storageState: AUTH_STATE_PATH })
+    await context.addInitScript((entries: Record<string, string>) => {
+      Object.entries(entries).forEach(([k, v]) => localStorage.setItem(k, v))
+    }, E2E_OVERLAY_SUPPRESSION)
+    sharedPage = await context.newPage()
+    await sharedPage.goto(AGENTS.fullstack_assistant.path, {
+      waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded',
+      timeout: TIMEOUTS.PAGE_LOAD,
+    })
+    // Wait for page to be ready
+    await expect(sharedPage.locator('[data-testid="genclaw-page-header"]')).toBeVisible({
+      timeout: TIMEOUTS.PAGE_LOAD,
+    })
+  })
+
+  test.afterAll(async () => {
+    await sharedPage?.context().close()
+  })
+
+  test('Scenario: Model selector button is visible with default model', async () => {
+    await test.step('Then the model selector button should be visible', async () => {
+      const selectorButton = sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-button')
+      await expect(selectorButton).toBeVisible({ timeout: TIMEOUTS.PAGE_LOAD })
+    })
+
+    await test.step('And it should display a model name', async () => {
+      const selectorButton = sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-button')
+      const buttonText = await selectorButton.innerText()
+      expect(buttonText.trim().length).toBeGreaterThan(0)
+    })
+  })
+
+  test('Scenario: Dropdown opens with model tabs and options', async () => {
+    await test.step('When clicking the model selector button', async () => {
+      await sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-button').click()
+    })
+
+    await test.step('Then the dropdown should appear', async () => {
+      await expect(sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-dropdown')).toBeVisible()
+    })
+
+    await test.step('And all three tabs should be visible', async () => {
+      await expect(sharedPage.getByTestId('model-tab-text')).toBeVisible()
+      await expect(sharedPage.getByTestId('model-tab-image')).toBeVisible()
+      await expect(sharedPage.getByTestId('model-tab-video')).toBeVisible()
+    })
+
+    await test.step('And at least one model option should be listed', async () => {
+      const options = sharedPage.locator('[data-testid^="model-option-"]')
+      const count = await options.count()
+      expect(count).toBeGreaterThan(0)
+    })
+
+    // Close dropdown for next test
+    await sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-button').click()
+    await expect(sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-dropdown')).toBeHidden()
+  })
+
+  test('Scenario: Tab switching filters model list', async () => {
+    await test.step('When opening the dropdown', async () => {
+      await sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-button').click()
+      await expect(sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-dropdown')).toBeVisible()
+    })
+
+    const modelCounts: Record<string, number> = {}
+
+    for (const tab of ['text', 'image', 'video'] as const) {
+      await test.step(`When clicking the ${tab} tab`, async () => {
+        await sharedPage.getByTestId(`model-tab-${tab}`).click()
+        await sharedPage.waitForTimeout(300)
+      })
+
+      await test.step(`Then the ${tab} tab should show its models`, async () => {
+        const options = sharedPage.locator('[data-testid^="model-option-"]')
+        modelCounts[tab] = await options.count()
+      })
+    }
+
+    await test.step('And at least one tab should have models', async () => {
+      const totalModels = Object.values(modelCounts).reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0)
+      expect(totalModels).toBeGreaterThan(0)
+    })
+
+    // Close dropdown
+    await sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-button').click()
+    await expect(sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-dropdown')).toBeHidden()
+  })
+
+  test('Scenario: Selecting a different model updates the button', async () => {
+    await test.step('When opening the dropdown', async () => {
+      await sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-button').click()
+      await expect(sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-dropdown')).toBeVisible()
+    })
+
+    let targetModelName = ''
+
+    await test.step('And clicking a non-selected model', async () => {
+      for (const tab of ['image', 'text', 'video'] as const) {
+        await sharedPage.getByTestId(`model-tab-${tab}`).click()
+        await sharedPage.waitForTimeout(300)
+
+        const allOptions = sharedPage.locator('[data-testid^="model-option-"]')
+        const optionCount = await allOptions.count()
+
+        for (let i = 0; i < optionCount; i++) {
+          const option = allOptions.nth(i)
+          if (await option.isDisabled()) continue
+
+          const isSelected = (await option.getAttribute('aria-selected')) === 'true'

+            break

+      if (!targetModelName) {

+        return

+      await expect(sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-dropdown')).toBeHidden()
+    })
+
+    await test.step('And the button should show the newly selected model', async () => {

+    })
+  })
+
+  test('Scenario: ESC key closes the dropdown', async () => {
+    await test.step('When opening the dropdown', async () => {
+      await sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-button').click()
+      await expect(sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-dropdown')).toBeVisible()
+    })
+
+    await test.step('And pressing Escape', async () => {
+      await sharedPage.keyboard.press('Escape')
+    })
+
+    await test.step('Then the dropdown should close', async () => {
+      await expect(sharedPage.getByTestId('model-selector-dropdown')).toBeHidden()
+    })
+  })
+})

+import { type Page } from '@playwright/test'
+import path from 'path'
+
+import { expect, test } from '../fixtures/base'
+import { E2E_OVERLAY_SUPPRESSION, TIMEOUTS } from '../fixtures/test-data'
+
+const AUTH_STATE_PATH = path.resolve(__dirname, '../.auth/user.json')
+const isStaging = process.env.E2E_ENV === 'staging'
+
+/**
+ * E2E: Onboarding Flow (Staging Only)
+ *
+ * Uses ?onboarding=preview mode to test the full onboarding UI
+ * without real backend API calls. Preview mode requires
+ * NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV === 'staging' (build-time env), so this
+ * spec only runs against staging deployments.
+ *
+ * Active steps (reminder & channel are currently hidden):
+ *   1. Invite Code — enter code + Continue
+ *   2. Name Input — enter name + Continue (or Skip)
+ *   3. Companion Select — carousel + Hire (or skip)
+ *   4. Loading — auto-completes after ~2.5s
+ */
+test.describe('Feature: Onboarding Flow', () => {
+  test.skip(!isStaging, 'Skipped — preview mode requires staging deployment (NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV=staging)')
+  test.describe.configure({ mode: 'serial' })
+
+  let sharedPage: Page
+
+  test.beforeAll(async ({ browser }) => {
+    const context = await browser.newContext({ storageState: AUTH_STATE_PATH })
+    // Shared auth state pre-marks onboarding as completed so other specs can
+    // skip the modal. This spec needs to start from step 1, so strip those
+    // keys before React reads them on first render.
+    await context.addInitScript(() => {
+      Object.keys(localStorage)
+        .filter((k) => k.startsWith('ecap:onboarding:progress'))
+        .forEach((k) => localStorage.removeItem(k))
+    })
+    // Suppress GuideTourModal + other overlays that block interaction on a fresh
+    // onboarding run (they sit at z-[100] above the modal and intercept clicks).
+    await context.addInitScript((entries: Record<string, string>) => {
+      Object.entries(entries).forEach(([k, v]) => localStorage.setItem(k, v))
+    }, E2E_OVERLAY_SUPPRESSION)
+    sharedPage = await context.newPage()
+  })
+
+  test.afterAll(async () => {
+    await sharedPage?.context().close()
+  })
+
+  test('Scenario: Onboarding modal opens in preview mode', async () => {
+    await test.step('When navigating to chat with onboarding=preview', async () => {
+      await sharedPage.goto('/en/chat?onboarding=preview', {
+        waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded',
+        timeout: TIMEOUTS.PAGE_LOAD,
+      })
+    })
+
+    await test.step('Then the onboarding modal should appear', async () => {
+      // Intro animation plays first (~2s), then invite code step appears
+      // Look for the invite code input as the definitive signal
+      await expect(sharedPage.locator('input[type="text"]:visible').first()).toBeVisible({
+        timeout: 15_000,
+      })
+    })
+  })
+
+  test('Scenario: Step 1 — Invite Code entry and advance to Name step', async () => {
+    const continueBtn = sharedPage.getByRole('button', { name: 'Continue' })
+
+    await test.step('When entering an invite code and clicking Continue', async () => {
+      // Wait for the invite code step's own prompt — signals intro is done and
+      // this specific step is mounted (not some other input in the page below).
+      await expect(sharedPage.getByText('Got an invite code?')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 })
+
+      // Use placeholder-unique selector; pressSequentially types real keystrokes
+      // so the controlled input's onChange (toUpperCase transform) actually fires.
+      const input = sharedPage.locator('input[placeholder="Enter code"]')
+      await input.click()
+      await input.pressSequentially('TESTCODE', { delay: 30 })
+
+      // Fail fast if state didn't sync — clearer error than a 180s click timeout.
+      await expect(continueBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 })
+      await continueBtn.click()
+    })
+
+    await test.step('Then it should advance to the Name step', async () => {
+      // Name step signature: "Skip for now" text appears (invite code step doesn't have it)
+      await expect(sharedPage.getByText('Skip for now')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 })
+    })
+  })
+
+  test('Scenario: Step 2 — Name Input and advance to Companion Select', async () => {
+    await test.step('When entering a name and clicking Continue', async () => {
+      const nameInput = sharedPage.locator('input[type="text"]:visible').first()
+      await nameInput.fill('E2E Test User')
+      await sharedPage.getByRole('button', { name: 'Continue' }).click()
+    })
+
+    await test.step('Then it should advance to the Companion Select step', async () => {
+      // Wait for either "Hire" button or "choose later" to confirm we're on this step
+      const hireButton = sharedPage.getByRole('button', { name: /Hire/i })
+      const skipLink = sharedPage.getByText("I'll choose later")
+      await expect(hireButton.or(skipLink).first()).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 })
+    })
+  })
+
+  test('Scenario: Step 3 — Companion Select advances to Loading', async () => {
+    await test.step('When clicking Hire on the active agent', async () => {
+      const hireButton = sharedPage.getByRole('button', { name: /Hire/i }).first()
+      await hireButton.click()
+    })
+
+    await test.step('Then it should advance to the loading step', async () => {
+      await expect(sharedPage.getByText('Entering ZooClaw')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 })
+    })
+  })
+
+  test('Scenario: Step 4 — Loading completes and modal closes', async () => {
+    await test.step('Then the loading step should auto-complete', async () => {
+      await expect(sharedPage.getByText('Entering ZooClaw')).toBeHidden({ timeout: 10_000 })
+    })
+
+    await test.step('And the chat interface should be accessible', async () => {
+      const pageHeader = sharedPage.locator('[data-testid="genclaw-page-header"]')
+      const chatInput = sharedPage.locator('[contenteditable="true"]')
+      await expect(pageHeader.or(chatInput).first()).toBeVisible({ timeout: TIMEOUTS.PAGE_LOAD })
+    })
+  })
+})

+
+    await test.step('Then the cancel confirmation modal should appear', async () => {
+      await expect(page.getByText(/Cancel.*\?/)).toBeVisible({ timeout: 3_000 })
+      await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Stay Together' })).toBeVisible()
+    })
+
+    await test.step('When clicking Stay Together', async () => {
+      await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Stay Together' }).click()
+    })
+
+    await test.step('Then the modal should close without canceling', async () => {
+      await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Stay Together' })).toBeHidden({ timeout: 3_000 })
+    })
+  })
+})

commit.message:
refactor(web): drop backend.ts BackendAPI aliases + promote duplicates gate (B3) (#1120)

## Summary
- Cleans the 6 legacy `*BackendAPI` alias exports in
`web/src/lib/api/backend.ts` — the sole content of knip's "duplicate
exports" baseline.
- Promotes the `duplicates` category to knip's hard gate in
`02-dead-code.sh` so new aliases fail CI.
- First content-cleanup PR of the B track.

## Scope
- `web/src/lib/api/backend.ts`: remove the 6 alias exports
(`callBackendAPI` = `callAPI`, etc). Only reference in src/ is
`backend.ts` itself.
- `web/tests/unit/lib/api/backend.unit.spec.ts`: remove the "backward
compatible exports" `describe` block (sole consumer of the aliases). 19
functional tests remain.
- `web/scripts/ci-lint/02-dead-code.sh`: add `duplicates` to knip
`--include`; header comment updated to match the new incremental-gate
roadmap.

## Why this was safe to drop
`grep -r '(call|get|post|put|delete|patch)BackendAPI' web/` returned
only:
1. `web/src/lib/api/backend.ts` — the 6 `export const` lines themselves
2. `web/tests/unit/lib/api/backend.unit.spec.ts` — the one test block
that asserted they === the canonical functions

No production code (src/ or tests/e2e/) imports the alias form. The
"向后兼容" comment suggests they were kept during a rename from `BackendAPI`
→ `API`; the rename is now fully absorbed.

## Local verification
- `pnpm test:unit tests/unit/lib/api/backend.unit.spec.ts` — **19 tests
pass**
- `WARN_ONLY=1 pnpm lint:ci` — **exit 0**; knip dep-health gate (now
including `duplicates`) reports 0 violations
- `pnpm lint` — clean (prettier fixed one trailing-newline issue
post-edit)

## Interaction with open PRs
- **#1115 (A1-PR2)** is in merge queue. B3 doesn't touch
`01-import-boundaries.sh`, the baseline JSON, or the workflow step, so
order-of-merge doesn't matter. Whichever lands first, the other rebases
cleanly.

## Test plan
- [x] 19 existing backend.ts unit tests pass
- [x] Orchestrator runs knip with `duplicates` included, 0 violations
- [ ] CI confirms above
- [ ] Reviewer validates that `backward compatible exports` describe was
the only test coupling to the aliases

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5,13 +5,11 @@
 # Config: web/knip.config.ts
 # Spec:   docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-web-dead-code.md
 #
-# B2 scope: hard-gate dep-health categories only. Content-level
-# categories (files / exports / types / duplicates) are reported to
-# stderr but do not fail the build. Each is promoted to hard-gate as
-# its dedicated cleanup PR lands:
-#   B3  → duplicates
-#   B5  → files
-#   B6+ → exports / types / nsExports / nsTypes / enumMembers
+# Hard-gate categories grow incrementally as each cleanup PR lands:
+#   B2  → dependencies / devDependencies / unlisted / binaries / unresolved
+#   B3  → + duplicates
+#   B5  → + files
+#   B6+ → + exports / types / nsExports / nsTypes / enumMembers
 #
 # Baseline for dep-health categories is absorbed via
 # `ignoreDependencies` / `ignoreBinaries` in knip.config.ts; anything
@@ -27,4 +25,4 @@ echo "────────────────────────
 # Hard gate: dep-health categories only.
 echo "── knip — dep-health hard gate ────────────────────────────────────"
 pnpm exec knip --reporter symbols \
-  --include dependencies,devDependencies,unlisted,binaries,optionalPeerDependencies,unresolved
+  --include dependencies,devDependencies,unlisted,binaries,optionalPeerDependencies,unresolved,duplicates

test(web): UsageCard 全面覆盖 (#894 Step 5 补) (#1119)

## Summary

Epic #894 Step 5 (#899) — 再补一个：\`UsageCard.tsx\` (249 LOC) 从 0% → 全分支，20
tests。

## 新增测试

| 组 | # | 覆盖 |
|---|---|---|
| 内容状态机 | 4 | error / no-data / loading / usage 呈现 |
| Stat tile 值 | 6 | messages user/asst 分解 / latency.count=0 → "—" /
ms→秒格式 (1.2s, p95 2.5s) / sessions / errors + "of N messages" /
toolCalls + uniqueTools |
| 日期预设 | 5 | default=today / yesterday / 7days / preset sticky / loading
→ refresh disabled |
| DailyStrip | 3 | empty / length=1 (阈值>1 隐藏) / length>=2 渲染 bar+tooltip
|
| TopTools | 2 | 空数组隐藏 / slice(0,5) 截断 |

## Harness 要点

- \`vi.setSystemTime(new Date('2026-04-20T12:00:00Z'))\` 固定让
\`dateRange()\` preset 派生日期确定
- i18n mock 返回 key，文案断言全走 key

## Bug-hunting

无新发现。状态机切换合理，preset sticky 逻辑对。

## Test plan

- [x] 20/20 通过
- [x] tsc clean
- [x] lint clean
- [ ] CI 绿

- Epic #894 Step 5 (#899) 
- 续 DiscordSetupWizard #1112 / ChannelsSection / Slack / Telegram /
Feishu / ModelSection / TimezoneSection / SaveButton (这些已有测试)

LOC) / DiaryCards (150 LOC) / ClawSettingsClient (438 LOC, key parts
~120)
