files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,257 @@
+// @vitest-environment jsdom
+import { cleanup, fireEvent, render } from '@testing-library/react'
+import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest'
+
+import type { BotSessionsUsageResponse } from '@/lib/api/openclaw-settings'
+
+// ── Hoisted mocks ──
+
+const hoisted = vi.hoisted(() => ({
+  useTranslation: vi.fn(),
+}))
+
+vi.mock('@/contexts/LanguageContext', () => ({
+  useTranslation: hoisted.useTranslation,
+}))
+
+import { UsageCard } from '@/app/[locale]/claw-settings/components/UsageCard'
+
+// ── Fixtures ──
+
+type Usage = BotSessionsUsageResponse
+
+function buildUsage(overrides: Partial<Usage['aggregates']> & { sessions?: Usage['sessions'] } = {}): Usage {
+  const defaults: Usage = {
+    startDate: '2026-04-20',
+    endDate: '2026-04-20',
+    aggregates: {
+      messages: { total: 100, user: 40, assistant: 60, toolCalls: 20, errors: 3 },
+      tools: { totalCalls: 20, uniqueTools: 4, tools: [] },
+      latency: { count: 10, avgMs: 1234, p95Ms: 2500, minMs: 100, maxMs: 3000 },
+      daily: [],
+    },
+    totals: { totalTokens: 5000, totalCost: 0.12 },
+    sessions: [{ sessionId: 's1' }, { sessionId: 's2' }],
+  }
+  // Pull `sessions` out before spreading so it doesn't leak into aggregates
+  // (TypeScript accepts the leak because Partial<aggregates> has no `sessions`,
+  // but runtime-wise the key would sneak in).
+  const { sessions, ...aggOverrides } = overrides
+  return {
+    ...defaults,
+    aggregates: { ...defaults.aggregates, ...aggOverrides },
+    sessions: sessions ?? defaults.sessions,
+  }
+}
+
+type Props = React.ComponentProps<typeof UsageCard>
+
+function buildProps(overrides: Partial<Props> = {}): Props {
+  return {
+    usage: null,
+    loading: false,
+    error: null,
+    onFetch: vi.fn(),
+    ...overrides,
+  }
+}
+
+describe('UsageCard', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    hoisted.useTranslation.mockReturnValue((key: string) => key)
+    // Install fake timers BEFORE setSystemTime — without this, Vitest's fake
+    // clock isn't active so `new Date()` in source still returns the real
+    // current date. The default preset date assertions below are hardcoded
+    // to 2026-04-20, which would start failing on 2026-04-21. Matches the
+    // pattern in sessionCache.unit.spec.ts + cron-client.unit.spec.tsx.
+    vi.useFakeTimers()
+    vi.setSystemTime(new Date('2026-04-20T12:00:00Z'))
+  })
+
+  afterEach(() => {
+    cleanup()
+    vi.useRealTimers()
+  })
+
+  // ── content state machine ──
+  describe('content states', () => {
+    it('error set → renders error message', () => {
+      const { getByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ error: 'Boom API 500' })} />)
+      expect(getByText('Boom API 500')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('no usage + !loading → shows "no data, click refresh"', () => {
+      const { getByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps()} />)
+      expect(getByText('settings.noDataClickRefresh')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('loading + no usage → shows loading text', () => {
+      const { getByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ loading: true })} />)
+      expect(getByText('common.loading')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('usage present → renders stat tile labels + NO loading/empty messages', () => {
+      const usage = buildUsage()
+      const { getByText, queryByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ usage })} />)
+      expect(getByText('settings.messages')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('settings.toolCalls')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('settings.latency')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('settings.errors')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('settings.statSessions')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(queryByText('common.loading')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(queryByText('settings.noDataClickRefresh')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── stat tile values ──
+  describe('stat tile values', () => {
+    it('messages total / user / assistant rendered', () => {
+      const usage = buildUsage({ messages: { total: 42, user: 10, assistant: 32, toolCalls: 5, errors: 2 } })
+      const { getByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ usage })} />)
+      expect(getByText('42')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('10 user · 32 asst')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('latency.count=0 → avg/p95 shown as "—" (both value and sub)', () => {
+      const usage = buildUsage({ latency: { count: 0, avgMs: 0, p95Ms: 0, minMs: 0, maxMs: 0 } })
+      const { getAllByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ usage })} />)
+      // Exactly two dashes: latValue (`—`) + latSub (`—`). Other tiles keep
+      // their numeric values because messages/tools/sessions are populated.
+      expect(getAllByText('—')).toHaveLength(2)
+    })
+
+    it('latency formats ms as "{seconds}s" with 1 decimal', () => {
+      const usage = buildUsage({ latency: { count: 5, avgMs: 2500, p95Ms: 4100, minMs: 100, maxMs: 5000 } })
+      const { getByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ usage })} />)
+      expect(getByText('2.5s')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('p95 4.1s')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('sessions count + "active in period" sub shown', () => {
+      // Default fixture has messages.errors=3; use 4 sessions to avoid two
+      // "3" text collisions when getByText asserts sessions tile value.
+      const usage = buildUsage({
+        sessions: [{ sessionId: 'a' }, { sessionId: 'b' }, { sessionId: 'c' }, { sessionId: 'd' }],
+      })
+      const { getByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ usage })} />)
+      expect(getByText('4')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('active in period')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('errors shown with "of {total} messages" sub', () => {
+      const usage = buildUsage({ messages: { total: 200, user: 90, assistant: 110, toolCalls: 30, errors: 7 } })
+      const { getByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ usage })} />)
+      expect(getByText('7')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('of 200 messages')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('toolCalls tile uses totalCalls + uniqueTools sub', () => {
+      const usage = buildUsage({ tools: { totalCalls: 55, uniqueTools: 8, tools: [] } })
+      const { getByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ usage })} />)
+      expect(getByText('55')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('8 tools used')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── preset buttons ──
+  describe('preset date buttons', () => {
+    it('default preset is "today" — click Refresh → onFetch called with today/today', () => {
+      const onFetch = vi.fn()
+      const { getByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ onFetch })} />)
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('↻'))
+      expect(onFetch).toHaveBeenCalledWith('2026-04-20', '2026-04-20')
+    })
+
+    it('click "yesterday" → onFetch with yesterday/yesterday dates', () => {
+      const onFetch = vi.fn()
+      const { getByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ onFetch })} />)
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('settings.yesterday'))
+      expect(onFetch).toHaveBeenCalledWith('2026-04-19', '2026-04-19')
+    })
+
+    it('click "7days" → onFetch with start=today-6 / end=today', () => {
+      const onFetch = vi.fn()
+      const { getByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ onFetch })} />)
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('settings.sevenDays'))
+      expect(onFetch).toHaveBeenCalledWith('2026-04-14', '2026-04-20')
+    })
+
+    it('after picking "yesterday", clicking Refresh re-fetches with yesterday range (preset is sticky)', () => {
+      const onFetch = vi.fn()
+      const { getByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ onFetch })} />)
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('settings.yesterday'))
+      onFetch.mockClear()
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('↻'))
+      expect(onFetch).toHaveBeenCalledWith('2026-04-19', '2026-04-19')
+    })
+
+    it('loading=true → refresh button disabled + shows "…"', () => {
+      const { getByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ loading: true })} />)
+      const refresh = getByText('…') as HTMLButtonElement
+      expect(refresh).toBeDisabled()
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── DailyStrip ──
+  describe('DailyStrip visibility', () => {
+    it('daily undefined/empty → no "Daily Activity" heading', () => {
+      const usage = buildUsage({ daily: [] })
+      const { queryByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ usage })} />)
+      expect(queryByText('settings.dailyActivity')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('daily length === 1 → strip hidden (threshold is > 1)', () => {
+      const usage = buildUsage({
+        daily: [{ date: '2026-04-20', tokens: 10, cost: 0.01, messages: 5, toolCalls: 1, errors: 0 }],
+      })
+      const { queryByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ usage })} />)
+      expect(queryByText('settings.dailyActivity')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('daily length >= 2 → strip renders with one bar per day', () => {
+      const usage = buildUsage({
+        daily: [
+          { date: '2026-04-14', tokens: 10, cost: 0.01, messages: 5, toolCalls: 1, errors: 0 },
+          { date: '2026-04-15', tokens: 20, cost: 0.02, messages: 10, toolCalls: 2, errors: 0 },
+          { date: '2026-04-16', tokens: 30, cost: 0.03, messages: 15, toolCalls: 3, errors: 1 },
+        ],
+      })
+      const { getByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ usage })} />)
+      expect(getByText('settings.dailyActivity')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('2026-04-14')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('5 msgs')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('15 msgs')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── TopTools ──
+  describe('TopTools visibility', () => {
+    it('tools array empty → heading NOT shown', () => {
+      const usage = buildUsage({ tools: { totalCalls: 0, uniqueTools: 0, tools: [] } })
+      const { queryByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ usage })} />)
+      expect(queryByText('settings.topTools')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('tools array non-empty → renders up to top 5 by order', () => {
+      const usage = buildUsage({
+        tools: {
+          totalCalls: 30,
+          uniqueTools: 6,
+          tools: [
+            { name: 'a', count: 10 },
+            { name: 'b', count: 7 },
+            { name: 'c', count: 5 },
+            { name: 'd', count: 4 },
+            { name: 'e', count: 3 },
+            { name: 'f', count: 1 }, // should be trimmed
+          ],
+        },
+      })
+      const { getByText, queryByText } = render(<UsageCard {...buildProps({ usage })} />)
+      expect(getByText('settings.topTools')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('a')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('e')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(queryByText('f')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+  })
+})

commit.message:
feat(web): freeze import-boundaries baseline + flip hard gate (A1-PR2) (#1115)

## Summary
- Merges original A1-PR2 (baseline) + A1-PR3 (hard gate) into one
rollout step — same 2-phase pattern as deptry / B2.
- Generates `.dependency-cruiser-known-violations.json` containing the
18 legacy violations frozen via `depcruise-baseline`.
- `01-import-boundaries.sh` drops `WARN_ONLY`, uses `--ignore-known` —
baseline passes, new violations fail CI.
- CI step no longer sets `WARN_ONLY=1`.

## Baseline contents

18 known violations (14 errors + 4 warnings) — same numbers reported in
#1098 warn-mode run:

| Contract | Count |
|----------|-------|
| W1 (lib pure) | 2 |
| W2 (hooks pure) | 3 |
| W3 (contexts pure) | 5 |
| W4 (components below pages) | 2 |
| W5 (feature isolation) | 4 |
| W6 (theme leaf) | 1 |

## Maintenance

Each A1-PR3+ cleanup PR removes a batch of entries from
`web/.dependency-cruiser-known-violations.json` and fixes the matching
imports. The file shrinks monotonically until empty.

Regenerate with:
```bash
pnpm exec depcruise-baseline --config .dependency-cruiser.cjs 'src/**/*.{ts,tsx}'
```

## Local verification
- `pnpm lint:imports` — exit 0, prints `18 known violations ignored`.
- A novel violation (e.g. `src/lib/foo.ts` importing `@/components/Bar`)
would fail the gate since it's not in the baseline.

## Spec changes
The PR matrix in
`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-web-import-boundaries.md` is updated
from 5 phases to 3 (PR0 spec / PR1 warn / PR2 baseline + hard gate /
PR3+ cleanup). Rationale: after PR1 landed, the `@/*` alias resolver
risk was confirmed resolved, so the extra "soft baseline freeze" step
between warn and hard gate added no safety margin. Mirrors the B track's
2-phase approach.

orchestrator` step because A1 was still in warn mode. Once this PR
lands, B2 rebase will drop that env block — the `WARN_ONLY` path is gone
from `01-import-boundaries.sh` and no longer needed.

passes on baseline, exit 0

unchanged

committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1776693679 +0000

feat(web): freeze import-boundaries baseline + flip hard gate (A1-PR2) (#1115)

## Summary
- Merges original A1-PR2 (baseline) + A1-PR3 (hard gate) into one
rollout step — same 2-phase pattern as deptry / B2.
- Generates `.dependency-cruiser-known-violations.json` containing the
18 legacy violations frozen via `depcruise-baseline`.
- `01-import-boundaries.sh` drops `WARN_ONLY`, uses `--ignore-known` —
baseline passes, new violations fail CI.
- CI step no longer sets `WARN_ONLY=1`.

## Baseline contents

18 known violations (14 errors + 4 warnings) — same numbers reported in
#1098 warn-mode run:

| Contract | Count |
|----------|-------|
| W1 (lib pure) | 2 |
| W2 (hooks pure) | 3 |
| W3 (contexts pure) | 5 |
| W4 (components below pages) | 2 |
| W5 (feature isolation) | 4 |
| W6 (theme leaf) | 1 |

## Maintenance

Each A1-PR3+ cleanup PR removes a batch of entries from
`web/.dependency-cruiser-known-violations.json` and fixes the matching
imports. The file shrinks monotonically until empty.

Regenerate with:
```bash
pnpm exec depcruise-baseline --config .dependency-cruiser.cjs 'src/**/*.{ts,tsx}'
```

## Local verification
- `pnpm lint:imports` — exit 0, prints `18 known violations ignored`.
- A novel violation (e.g. `src/lib/foo.ts` importing `@/components/Bar`)
would fail the gate since it's not in the baseline.

The PR matrix in
`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-web-import-boundaries.md` is updated
from 5 phases to 3 (PR0 spec / PR1 warn / PR2 baseline + hard gate /

commit.message:
ci: skip redundant gating on push:main in code-quality.yml (#1038) (#1111)

## Context

Closes #1038. After merge queue was enabled, every PR merge produces
**two** `code-quality` runs against the same commit SHA:

1. **merge_group** (gating, before fast-forward) — must pass to allow
merge
2. **push:main** (after fast-forward) — duplicates all gating, and
occasionally red-crosses main from unrelated flakes

Example: PR #994's merge produced runs
[24558845557](https://github.com/SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace/actions/runs/24558845557)
(merge_group ✅) and
[24559126136](https://github.com/SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace/actions/runs/24559126136)
(push ❌, Simulator CoreData teardown flake).

## Approach (issue option A — minimal)

Guard pure gating steps with `if: github.event_name != 'push'` so they
skip on main-branch pushes. Test + coverage + badge publishing steps are
untouched so the iOS/web coverage badges keep updating on every merge.

### Affected steps

| Job | Gating steps now skipped on `push:main` |
|---|---|
| `web-quality` | Lint, ignores SHRINK-ONLY check, Import boundaries
(warn-mode), Type check, jscpd src + tests |
| `ios-quality` | SwiftLint, jscpd src + tests |
| `python-duplication-check` | entire job (guarded at job level) |
| `claw-interface-quality` | entire reusable-workflow job (guarded at
job level) |

The aggregator `code-quality` step is updated to treat `skipped` status
on python jobs as non-failure (previously required `success`).

## Intentionally not addressed

- **iOS unit tests still run on push:main**, because they produce the
coverage artifact the iOS badge step consumes. A Simulator flake during
that step can still red-cross main (which is **issue #1038 acceptance
item 3**). Fixing that requires moving iOS test + badge publishing into
the `merge_group` event instead of `push:main`, which is a larger
architectural change. Tracked in #1116.
- **`on: push: branches: [main]` trigger kept intentionally** so a
manual/bypass push still triggers a run (not a silent no-op). The
improvement comes from step-level skipping, not trigger removal.

## Verification

`act` isn't available in this worktree, so the real verification is the
first post-merge `push:main` run: look for Lint / tsc / jscpd /
SwiftLint rows marked `skipped` in the `ios-quality` + `web-quality`
jobs under the main-branch run.

Meanwhile this PR's own CI (which runs under `pull_request` event)
executes the **full** gating path — confirming the added `if` conditions
don't break the PR-time gate.

## Test plan

- [x] YAML structure reviewed for unintended step ordering / missing-if
errors
- [x] `code-quality` aggregator updated so `python*` jobs in `skipped`
state don't falsely fail
- [ ] Post-merge verification: first `push:main` run after merge should
show Lint/tsc/jscpd/SwiftLint as `skipped`, with `Unit Tests with
Coverage` + badge steps still running

Closes #1038

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -134,11 +134,16 @@ jobs:
       - name: Install dependencies
         run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
 
+      # Gating steps — skipped on `push: main` because merge_group already
+      # gated them before the fast-forward. Keeps main-branch runs focused on
+      # coverage badge publishing.
       - name: Lint (ESLint)
+        if: github.event_name != 'push'
         working-directory: web
         run: pnpm lint
 
       - name: ignores SHRINK-ONLY check (react/forbid-dom-props)
+        if: github.event_name != 'push'

+        if: github.event_name != 'push'
         working-directory: web
         env:
           WARN_ONLY: '1'   # A1 rollout: soft-gates 01 only; 02 is hard-enforced
         run: pnpm lint:ci
 
       - name: Type check (TypeScript)
+        if: github.event_name != 'push'
         working-directory: web
         run: npx tsc --noEmit
 
@@ -189,9 +196,11 @@ jobs:
         run: npm install -g jscpd@4
 
       - name: Duplication check — web source (threshold 6%)
+        if: github.event_name != 'push'
         run: jscpd -c web/.jscpd.src.json
 
       - name: Duplication check — web tests (threshold 10%)
+        if: github.event_name != 'push'
         run: jscpd -c web/.jscpd.tests.json
 
       - name: Web Duplication Summary
@@ -280,6 +289,7 @@ jobs:

       - name: SwiftLint

         working-directory: ios/ZooClaw
         run: swiftlint --strict
 
@@ -399,9 +409,11 @@ jobs:
         run: npm install -g jscpd@4
 
       - name: Duplication check — iOS source (threshold 3%)
+        if: github.event_name != 'push'
         run: jscpd -c ios/.jscpd.src.json
 
       - name: Duplication check — iOS tests (threshold 8%)
+        if: github.event_name != 'push'
         run: jscpd -c ios/.jscpd.tests.json
 
       - name: iOS Duplication Summary
@@ -449,7 +461,7 @@ jobs:
 
   python-duplication-check:
     needs: [size, changes]
-    if: needs.changes.outputs.python == 'true' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
+    if: (needs.changes.outputs.python == 'true' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch') && github.event_name != 'push'
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     concurrency:
       group: python-duplication-${{ github.event_name }}-${{ github.ref }}
@@ -493,7 +505,7 @@ jobs:
 
   claw-interface-quality:
     needs: [size, changes]
-    if: needs.changes.outputs.python == 'true' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
+    if: (needs.changes.outputs.python == 'true' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch') && github.event_name != 'push'
     uses: SerendipityOneInc/srp-actions/.github/workflows/python-code-quality-v3.yml@main
     secrets: inherit
     with:
@@ -548,11 +560,14 @@ jobs:
             echo "❌ iOS quality check failed"
             exit 1
           fi
-          if [[ "$python_changed" == "true" && "$python" != "success" ]]; then
+          # `skipped` is expected on push: main (we intentionally skip redundant
+          # gating on main-branch pushes and let merge_group be the source of
+          # truth). Treat skipped as non-failure.
+          if [[ "$python_changed" == "true" && "$python" != "success" && "$python" != "skipped" ]]; then
             echo "❌ Claw-interface quality check failed"
             exit 1
           fi
-          if [[ "$python_changed" == "true" && "$python_dup" != "success" ]]; then
+          if [[ "$python_changed" == "true" && "$python_dup" != "success" && "$python_dup" != "skipped" ]]; then
             echo "❌ Python duplication check failed"
             exit 1
           fi

## Summary

PR 补 Discord。
20 tests / +320 source LOC from 0% → 全分支。

## 新增 20 个测试

| 分组 | # | 覆盖点 |
|---|---|---|
| welcome step | 3 | 渲染 / Cancel → onClose / getStarted 推进 |
| create-app step | 3 | 6 步说明 + developer portal link target/rel / back
→ welcome / next → input-token |
| input-token validation | 4 | 空 token / <50 字符 invalid hint / >=50
enabled / back 清 error |
| advanced config toggle | 3 | 折叠默认 / 展开后 dm/group select + account
input / 透传到 addClawChannel payload |
| handleConnect | 5 | 成功路径 + addClawChannel 默认参数 / Error 实例 message 显示 /
非 Error fallback / account 空白→"default" / token trim |
| invite step | 2 | 5 步 invite 说明 / Done → onSuccess+onClose |

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,297 @@
+// @vitest-environment jsdom
+import { cleanup, fireEvent, render, waitFor } from '@testing-library/react'
+import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest'
+
+// ── Hoisted mocks ──
+
+const hoisted = vi.hoisted(() => ({
+  useTranslation: vi.fn(),
+  addClawChannel: vi.fn(),
+}))
+
+vi.mock('@/contexts/LanguageContext', () => ({
+  useTranslation: hoisted.useTranslation,
+}))
+vi.mock('@/lib/api/openclaw-settings', () => ({
+  addClawChannel: hoisted.addClawChannel,
+}))
+
+import { DiscordSetupWizard } from '@/app/[locale]/claw-settings/components/DiscordSetupWizard'
+
+type Props = React.ComponentProps<typeof DiscordSetupWizard>
+
+function buildProps(overrides: Partial<Props> = {}): Props {
+  return {
+    uid: 'user-1',
+    onClose: vi.fn(),
+    onSuccess: vi.fn(),
+    ...overrides,
+  }
+}
+
+// Discord bot tokens are always > 50 chars in practice. Source validates
+// trim().length >= 50; use a fixture that just clears the threshold.
+const VALID_TOKEN = 'MTIz'.repeat(15) // 60 chars, passes the >= 50 gate
+
+/** Walk from welcome → input-token step. */
+function gotoInputToken(r: ReturnType<typeof render>) {
+  fireEvent.click(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.getStarted'))
+  fireEvent.click(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.next'))
+}
+
+describe('DiscordSetupWizard', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    hoisted.useTranslation.mockReturnValue((key: string) => key)
+    hoisted.addClawChannel.mockResolvedValue({ success: true })
+  })
+
+  afterEach(() => {
+    cleanup()
+    vi.restoreAllMocks()
+  })
+
+  // ── welcome step ──
+  describe('welcome step', () => {
+    it('renders welcome title + Cancel + getStarted', () => {
+      const { getByText } = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      expect(getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.welcomeTitle')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('clawSettings.channels.cancel')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.getStarted')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('click Cancel → onClose', () => {
+      const onClose = vi.fn()
+      const { getByText } = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps({ onClose })} />)
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('clawSettings.channels.cancel'))
+      expect(onClose).toHaveBeenCalledOnce()
+    })
+
+    it('click getStarted → advances to create-app', () => {
+      const { getByText } = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.getStarted'))
+      expect(getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.createAppTitle')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── create-app step ──
+  describe('create-app step', () => {
+    it('shows 6 numbered instruction steps + developer portal link', () => {
+      const { getByText, container } = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.getStarted'))
+      for (let i = 1; i <= 6; i++) {
+        expect(getByText(`clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.createAppStep${i}`)).toBeInTheDocument()
+      }
+      const link = container.querySelector('a[href="https://discord.com/developers/applications"]')
+      expect(link).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(link).toHaveAttribute('target', '_blank')
+      expect(link).toHaveAttribute('rel', 'noopener noreferrer')
+    })
+
+    it('click back → returns to welcome', () => {
+      const { getByText } = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.getStarted'))
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.back'))
+      expect(getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.welcomeTitle')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('click next → advances to input-token', () => {
+      const { getByText } = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.getStarted'))
+      fireEvent.click(getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.next'))
+      expect(getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.tokenTitle')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── input-token step ──
+  describe('input-token step — validation', () => {
+    it('empty token → connect disabled, no invalid-token hint', () => {
+      const r = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      gotoInputToken(r)
+      const connect = r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.connect') as HTMLButtonElement
+      expect(connect).toBeDisabled()
+      expect(r.queryByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.tokenInvalid')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('token shorter than 50 chars → invalid-token hint + connect disabled', () => {
+      const r = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      gotoInputToken(r)
+      const input = r.container.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement
+      fireEvent.change(input, { target: { value: 'tooshort' } })
+      expect(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.tokenInvalid')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.connect')).toBeDisabled()
+    })
+
+    it('token >= 50 chars → connect enabled, no invalid hint', () => {
+      const r = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      gotoInputToken(r)
+      const input = r.container.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement
+      fireEvent.change(input, { target: { value: VALID_TOKEN } })
+      expect(r.queryByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.tokenInvalid')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.connect')).not.toBeDisabled()
+    })
+
+    it('back button → returns to create-app AND clears any prior error', async () => {
+      hoisted.addClawChannel.mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error('boom'))
+      const r = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      gotoInputToken(r)
+      const input = r.container.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement
+      fireEvent.change(input, { target: { value: VALID_TOKEN } })
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.connect'))
+      await waitFor(() => expect(r.getByText('boom')).toBeInTheDocument())
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.back'))
+      // Advance back forward into input-token — prior error string should be gone.
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.next'))
+      expect(r.queryByText('boom')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── advanced config ──
+  describe('input-token step — advanced config toggle', () => {
+    it('collapsed by default: dmPolicy / groupPolicy / accountId inputs not shown', () => {
+      const r = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      gotoInputToken(r)
+      expect(r.queryByText('clawSettings.channels.dmPolicy')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(r.queryByText('clawSettings.channels.groupPolicy')).not.toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('click Advanced → reveals dm/group policy selects + account input', () => {
+      const r = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      gotoInputToken(r)
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText(/clawSettings\.channels\.discordSetup\.advancedConfig/))
+      expect(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.dmPolicy')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      expect(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.groupPolicy')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('advanced fields propagate into the addClawChannel payload', async () => {
+      const onSuccess = vi.fn()
+      const r = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps({ onSuccess })} />)
+      gotoInputToken(r)
+      const tokenInput = r.container.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement
+      fireEvent.change(tokenInput, { target: { value: VALID_TOKEN } })
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText(/clawSettings\.channels\.discordSetup\.advancedConfig/))
+
+      fireEvent.change(r.getByTestId('discord-account-input'), { target: { value: 'guild-42' } })
+
+      const selects = r.container.querySelectorAll('select')
+      fireEvent.change(selects[0], { target: { value: 'pairing' } })
+      fireEvent.change(selects[1], { target: { value: 'allowlist' } })
+
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.connect'))
+      await waitFor(() =>
+        expect(hoisted.addClawChannel).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+          'user-1',
+          expect.objectContaining({
+            platform: 'discord',
+            account: 'guild-42',
+            dm_policy: 'pairing',
+            group_policy: 'allowlist',
+            config: { token: VALID_TOKEN },
+          }),
+        ),
+      )
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── handleConnect API flow ──
+  describe('handleConnect', () => {
+    it('happy path: spinner → invite step; addClawChannel called with defaults', async () => {
+      const r = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      gotoInputToken(r)
+      const input = r.container.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement
+      fireEvent.change(input, { target: { value: VALID_TOKEN } })
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.connect'))
+      // Spinner shows the configuring title during the await
+      expect(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.configuringTitle')).toBeInTheDocument()
+      await waitFor(() => expect(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.inviteTitle')).toBeInTheDocument())
+      expect(hoisted.addClawChannel).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+        'user-1',
+        expect.objectContaining({
+          platform: 'discord',
+          account: 'default',
+          dm_policy: 'open',
+          group_policy: 'open',
+          allow_from: ['*'],
+          config: { token: VALID_TOKEN },
+        }),
+      )
+    })
+
+    it('API rejects with Error → step returns to input-token + error.message shown', async () => {
+      hoisted.addClawChannel.mockRejectedValue(new Error('Invalid bot token'))
+      const r = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      gotoInputToken(r)
+      const input = r.container.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement
+      fireEvent.change(input, { target: { value: VALID_TOKEN } })
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.connect'))
+      await waitFor(() => expect(r.getByText('Invalid bot token')).toBeInTheDocument())
+      // Still on input-token step
+      expect(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.tokenTitle')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    })
+
+    it('API rejects with non-Error → fallback "Failed to configure channel" message', async () => {
+      hoisted.addClawChannel.mockRejectedValue('string-rejection')
+      const r = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      gotoInputToken(r)
+      const input = r.container.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement
+      fireEvent.change(input, { target: { value: VALID_TOKEN } })
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.connect'))
+      await waitFor(() => expect(r.getByText('Failed to configure channel')).toBeInTheDocument())
+    })
+
+    it('account whitespace-empty → defaults to "default" in payload', async () => {
+      const r = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      gotoInputToken(r)
+      const tokenInput = r.container.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement
+      fireEvent.change(tokenInput, { target: { value: VALID_TOKEN } })
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText(/clawSettings\.channels\.discordSetup\.advancedConfig/))
+      fireEvent.change(r.getByTestId('discord-account-input'), { target: { value: '' } })
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.connect'))
+      await waitFor(() =>
+        expect(hoisted.addClawChannel).toHaveBeenCalledWith('user-1', expect.objectContaining({ account: 'default' })),
+      )
+    })
+
+    it('token trimmed before sending to API', async () => {
+      const r = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      gotoInputToken(r)
+      const input = r.container.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement
+      fireEvent.change(input, { target: { value: `   ${VALID_TOKEN}   ` } })
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.connect'))
+      await waitFor(() =>
+        expect(hoisted.addClawChannel).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+          'user-1',
+          expect.objectContaining({ config: { token: VALID_TOKEN } }),
+        ),
+      )
+    })
+  })
+
+  // ── invite + complete steps ──
+  describe('invite step', () => {
+    it('renders inviteTitle + 5 numbered invite steps', async () => {
+      const r = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps()} />)
+      gotoInputToken(r)
+      const input = r.container.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement
+      fireEvent.change(input, { target: { value: VALID_TOKEN } })
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.connect'))
+      await waitFor(() => r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.inviteTitle'))
+      for (let i = 1; i <= 5; i++) {
+        expect(r.getByText(`clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.inviteStep${i}`)).toBeInTheDocument()
+      }
+    })
+
+    it('click Done on invite → onSuccess + onClose fire together', async () => {
+      const onSuccess = vi.fn()
+      const onClose = vi.fn()
+      const r = render(<DiscordSetupWizard {...buildProps({ onSuccess, onClose })} />)
+      gotoInputToken(r)
+      const input = r.container.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement
+      fireEvent.change(input, { target: { value: VALID_TOKEN } })
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.connect'))
+      await waitFor(() => r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.inviteTitle'))
+      fireEvent.click(r.getByText('clawSettings.channels.discordSetup.done'))
+      expect(onSuccess).toHaveBeenCalledOnce()
+      expect(onClose).toHaveBeenCalledOnce()
+    })
+  })
+})

commit.message:
test(web): GenClawClient 内部组件覆盖 (#894 Step 4) (#1110)

## Summary

Epic #894 Step 4 (#898)。\`GenClawClient.tsx\` 默认导出 1100+ LOC 是整合组件（hooks
+ 布局 + provider 链），走 E2E 更合适。本 PR 聚焦 issue #898
明确点名的两块（**ChatErrorBoundary** + **连接错误 modal**），加上几个纯 UI helper，从
GenClawClient.tsx 导出内部组件让它们可独立受测。

## 源码变动（极小）

- 8 处 \`function\` → \`export function\` / \`class\` → \`export class\`
- 零语义变化、零 runtime 行为变化

## 新增 48 个测试

| 组件 | # | 覆盖点 |
|---|---|---|
| \`formatChannelTime\` | 8 | pure function — 空值 / NaN / 秒 vs 毫秒 自动缩放 /
分钟/小时/天分段 |
| \`ChannelIcon\` | 7 | 6 种平台 emoji 渲染 / 未知平台 SVG fallback /
case-insensitive |
| \`RemoteStatusBadge\` | 8 | 5 种 status → dot 颜色 / unknown fallback /
waiting pulse / message vs status 降级 |
| \`Toast\` | 4 | 渲染 + 4000ms 自动 dismiss / X 按钮立即关闭 / unmount 清 timer |
| \`ConfirmModal\` | 6 | open=false 不渲染 / 默认/自定义 labels /
onConfirm/onCancel / typeToConfirm gate / destructive variant / close 重置
typed |
| \`AdvancedRecreate\` | 5 | 展开/收起 / RECREATE 输入门 / onRecreate 触发 /
Cancel 路径 |
| \`ConnectionErrorModal\` | 5 | headline + status badge / wsError 段 /
reconnect 按钮 / redeploy→ConfirmModal→Confirm / redeploy Cancel |
| \`ChatErrorBoundary\` | 3 | 无错误透传子组件 / getDerivedStateFromError 返回空
partial / componentDidCatch 打 logger.warn + setState updater 增 errorKey
|

## 测试策略

### ChatErrorBoundary — 直接调类方法
React 19 concurrent replay 会同步重放 render 跳过正常 commit 路径，用 \`throw\`
驱动错误边界会 flaky。改为：直接 \`new ChatErrorBoundary()\`，spy setState，调
componentDidCatch 和 getDerivedStateFromError — 100% 确定。

### 其他内部组件 — render + testid + 行为断言
ConfirmModal 里的 "Confirm"/"Cancel" 按钮实际 label 是 t(key) ||
'fallback'。i18n mock 返回 key，所以 DOM 上显示 \`common.cancel\` /
\`genClaw.recreateClaw\`。getAllByText 处理同 label 多元素（modal h3 + button
同文本）。

## 不在本 PR 范围

- **auth 检查分支**：位于默认导出内部（async useEffect 里校 uid + router.push）。要测就得 mock
60+ 依赖或直接渲染默认导出 — 性价比太低，issue 里"auth 检查"留给 E2E 或后续独立 PR。
- **useEffect 里抛错是否被 ErrorBoundary 捕获**：issue bug-hunting 里提到；已在
ChatErrorBoundary 说明 componentDidCatch 只捕 render/children 抛错，effect 抛错是
React 已知 gotcha，不在本 PR 范围。

## Test plan

- [x] 48/48 GenClawClient.internals 测试通过
- [x] \`npx tsc --noEmit\` clean
- [x] \`pnpm lint\` clean
- [ ] CI 绿

## 关联

- Epic #894 Step 4 (#898)
- #898 还剩 \`GenClawClient.tsx\` 默认导出的 auth 检查分支（超出本 PR），建议等 E2E 补

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

partial / componentDidCatch 打 logger.warn + setState updater 增 errorKey
|

## 测试策略

### ChatErrorBoundary — 直接调类方法
React 19 concurrent replay 会同步重放 render 跳过正常 commit 路径，用 \`throw\`
驱动错误边界会 flaky。改为：直接 \`new ChatErrorBoundary()\`，spy setState，调
componentDidCatch 和 getDerivedStateFromError — 100% 确定。

### 其他内部组件 — render + testid + 行为断言
ConfirmModal 里的 "Confirm"/"Cancel" 按钮实际 label 是 t(key) ||
'fallback'。i18n mock 返回 key，所以 DOM 上显示 \`common.cancel\` /
\`genClaw.recreateClaw\`。getAllByText 处理同 label 多元素（modal h3 + button
同文本）。

## 不在本 PR 范围

- **auth 检查分支**：位于默认导出内部（async useEffect 里校 uid + router.push）。要测就得 mock
60+ 依赖或直接渲染默认导出 — 性价比太低，issue 里"auth 检查"留给 E2E 或后续独立 PR。
- **useEffect 里抛错是否被 ErrorBoundary 捕获**：issue bug-hunting 里提到；已在
ChatErrorBoundary 说明 componentDidCatch 只捕 render/children 抛错，effect 抛错是
React 已知 gotcha，不在本 PR 范围。

## Test plan

- [x] 48/48 GenClawClient.internals 测试通过
- [x] \`npx tsc --noEmit\` clean
- [x] \`pnpm lint\` clean
- [ ] CI 绿

- Epic #894 Step 4 (#898)

+      const instance = new ChatErrorBoundary({ children: null })
+      const setStateSpy = vi.spyOn(instance, 'setState').mockImplementation(() => {})
+      instance.componentDidCatch(new Error('boom'))
+
+      expect(hoisted.logger.warn).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.stringContaining('ChatErrorBoundary caught'), 'boom')
+      expect(setStateSpy).toHaveBeenCalledOnce()
+      // The updater callback bumps errorKey by 1.
+      const updater = setStateSpy.mock.calls[0][0] as (prev: { errorKey: number }) => { errorKey: number }
+      expect(updater({ errorKey: 5 })).toEqual({ errorKey: 6 })
+    })
+  })
+})

commit.message:
feat(web): wire knip for dead code / dep health gate (B2) (#1106)

## Summary
- Wires **knip** as a hard gate for dep-health categories in `web/` CI.
- Content-level categories (files / exports / types / duplicates) are
reported informationally; hard-gated in later B-track PRs.
- Implements all three rounds of #1095 review feedback directly.
- B2 scope: **install + baseline freeze + hard gate for dep-health
only**.

## Baseline (absorbed in `knip.config.ts`)

| Category | Count | Handling |
|----------|-------|----------|
| Unused dependencies | 7 | `ignoreDependencies` baseline legacy — B4
cleans |
| Unlisted dependencies | 1 (`postcss`) | `ignoreDependencies` — B4 adds
explicitly |
| Unlisted binaries | 1 (`tsx`) | `ignoreBinaries` — B4 `pnpm add -D
tsx` |
| Permanent FPs | 2 | `eslint-config-next` + `@vitest/expect` — kept
separate |
| Unused files | 28 | Informational (B5) |
| Unused exports | 39 | Informational (B6+) |
| Unused types | 58 | Informational (B6+) |
| Duplicate exports | 6 | Informational (B3 first target) |

`pnpm lint:deadcode` exit 0 locally. Dep-health hard gate clean: 0
violations outside the allowlist.

## #1095 review feedback addressed in this PR

From round 2:
- ✅ `eslint-config-next` as permanent FP (FlatCompat string ref)
- ✅ `@vitest/expect` as permanent FP (`declare module` augmentation in
`web/jest-dom.d.ts:20`)
- ✅ App Router metadata entries (`robots.ts` + preventive `sitemap` /
`manifest` / OG images / icons)
- ✅ knip version pinned to exact `6.5.0` (no caret)

From round 3:
- ✅ `project` glob includes `.mts`
- ✅ `tsx` correctly scoped to `ignoreBinaries` (rather than
`ignoreDependencies`)
- ✅ Independent `02-dead-code.sh` script, decoupled from A1's
`00-run-all.sh` to avoid merge-order coupling between A track and B
track

## Rollout contract
B2 gate covers: `dependencies / devDependencies / unlisted / binaries /
unresolved / optionalPeerDependencies`. Content categories ship in:
- **B3** → promote `duplicates` to hard gate + clean 6 in
`src/lib/api/backend.ts`
- **B5** → promote `files` + delete 28 unused files
- **B6+** → promote `exports / types / enumMembers / ...`
module-by-module

Each category-promotion PR adds its type to the `--include` list in
`02-dead-code.sh` and removes the corresponding allowlist entries.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm lint:deadcode` exit 0 locally
- [x] Full report prints 131 informational items then dep-health gate
prints 0 violations
- [ ] CI `code-quality / web-quality` runs new step, gate passes
- [ ] Existing ESLint / TS / unit-test / jscpd steps unchanged
- [ ] Reviewer validates `ignoreDependencies` permanent vs legacy split
is correctly commented

## Notes
- Runs independently of A1-PR1 (#1098). Both PRs add files under
`web/scripts/ci-lint/` but non-overlapping (`02-*` here, `00-*` / `01-*`
there); git auto-merge should resolve cleanly.
- `@sentry/cloudflare` in the legacy list with a comment — may resolve
via `src/instrumentation.ts`; B4 to verify before removing.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,71 @@
+/**
+ * knip config — web/ cross-file dead code / dep health gate
+ *
+ * Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-web-dead-code.md
+ *
+ * knip's built-in plugins (Next.js / Vitest / Playwright / ESLint /
+ * Prettier / TypeScript / Tailwind / Postcss) auto-detect most entry
+ * points. We only declare entries that plugins don't cover.
+ *
+ * Scope:
+ *   Detects unused files / exports / types / deps / unlisted deps /
+ *   duplicates / unlisted binaries across src/ and tests/.
+ *
+ * Baseline (B2): legacy violations are frozen via `ignoreDependencies`
+ * / `ignore` / `ignoreBinaries`. B3..B6+ PRs clean them
+ * category-by-category. Permanent FPs (non-discoverable statically)
+ * are kept separate from baseline legacy.
+ */
+import type { KnipConfig } from 'knip'
+
+const config: KnipConfig = {
+  // Entries NOT auto-detected by plugins:
+  entry: [
+    // App Router metadata files (implicit Next.js entries) — `robots.ts`
+    // exists today; the rest are preventive so future additions don't
+    // trigger spurious "unused file" reports.
+    'src/app/**/{robots,sitemap,manifest}.ts',
+    'src/app/**/{opengraph-image,twitter-image}.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}',
+    'src/app/**/{icon,apple-icon}.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}',
+  ],
+  project: [
+    'src/**/*.{ts,tsx,js,mjs,mts}',
+    'tests/**/*.{ts,tsx}',
+  ],
+  ignore: [
+    'src/locales/**', // auto-generated translation files
+  ],
+  ignoreDependencies: [
+    // ─── Permanent exemptions (known FPs — cannot be detected statically) ───
+    // eslint-config-next is referenced via FlatCompat.extends('next/...') string
+    // ref in web/eslint.config.mjs. knip cannot follow string references to
+    // package names. Deleting it silently breaks ESLint.
+    'eslint-config-next',
+    // @vitest/expect is used via `declare module '@vitest/expect'` module
+    // augmentation in web/jest-dom.d.ts. knip does not analyze .d.ts
+    // augmentation. Deleting it silently breaks vitest type inference.
+    '@vitest/expect',
+    // dependency-cruiser is invoked by web/scripts/ci-lint/01-import-boundaries.sh
+    // via `pnpm exec`. knip does not analyze shell scripts for binary/package
+    // usage, so it flags the package as unused.
+    'dependency-cruiser',
+
+    // ─── Baseline legacy — B4 cleans one at a time. Each entry removed ─────
+    //     from this list == one commit fixing the corresponding code.
+    '@mdx-js/loader',
+    '@mdx-js/react',
+    '@next/mdx',
+    '@types/mdx',
+    '@stripe/stripe-js',
+    '@sentry/cloudflare', // verify via src/instrumentation.ts before removing
+    '@types/dompurify',
+    // Unlisted deps (B4 fix: pnpm add postcss):
+    'postcss',
+  ],
+  ignoreBinaries: [
+    // Unlisted binary (B4 fix: pnpm add -D tsx):
+    'tsx',
+  ],
+}
+
+export default config

@@ -26,6 +26,7 @@
 		"start": "next start",
 		"lint": "eslint src/ tests/ --quiet",
 		"lint:imports": "bash scripts/ci-lint/01-import-boundaries.sh",
+		"lint:deadcode": "bash scripts/ci-lint/02-dead-code.sh",

@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# web/scripts/ci-lint/02-dead-code.sh
+#
+# Enforces cross-file dead code + dep health gate via knip.
+# Config: web/knip.config.ts
+# Spec:   docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-web-dead-code.md
+#
+# B2 scope: hard-gate dep-health categories only. Content-level
+# categories (files / exports / types / duplicates) are reported to
+# stderr but do not fail the build. Each is promoted to hard-gate as
+# its dedicated cleanup PR lands:
+#   B3  → duplicates
+#   B5  → files
+#   B6+ → exports / types / nsExports / nsTypes / enumMembers
+#
+# Baseline for dep-health categories is absorbed via
+# `ignoreDependencies` / `ignoreBinaries` in knip.config.ts; anything
+# outside the allowlist fails CI.
+set -euo pipefail
+cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.."
+
+# Informational: report all categories (does not affect build status).
+echo "── knip — full report (informational) ─────────────────────────────"
+pnpm exec knip --reporter symbols --no-exit-code || true
+echo "───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
+
+# Hard gate: dep-health categories only.
+echo "── knip — dep-health hard gate ────────────────────────────────────"
+pnpm exec knip --reporter symbols \
+  --include dependencies,devDependencies,unlisted,binaries,optionalPeerDependencies,unresolved

commit.message:
chore(web): promote tests/ no-unused-vars from warn to error (#1073) (#1114)

Follow-up to #1109, which cleared the 23-warning backlog.

With the backlog at zero, flipping severity to `error` is the missing
piece — the lint script runs `eslint src/ tests/ --quiet`, which
silently filters warnings out of CI output, so warn-level here was
effectively a no-op. Error-level means future regressions actually break
the build.

## Summary

- `web/eslint.config.mjs` `tests/` override:
`@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` from `'warn'` → `'error'`
- Kept the existing `^_`-prefix ignore patterns untouched (so
deliberately-unused destructures like `_setMessages` still pass)

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm --filter web lint` — clean (exit 0)
- [x] Quick sanity: manually introduced a bogus unused var locally,
confirmed lint now fails
- [x] Depends on #1109 being merged to main (confirmed at the time of
branching this PR off `origin/main`)

Closes #1073

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore(web): clean up 23 no-unused-vars warnings in tests/ (#1109)

Preparatory cleanup so the follow-up PR can promote
`@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` from `warn` to `error` in the
`tests/` override without CI going red. Currently silenced by `eslint
src/ tests/ --quiet` in the `lint` script, so warnings have been
accumulating invisibly.

## Summary

- 3 unused imports: `beforeEach`, `waitFor`, `AuthSubscribe` type
- 1 motion stub destructure in PaywallContent: rename
`initial`/`animate`/`exit`/`transition` to underscore-prefixed so they
still get stripped out of DOM props but satisfy the `/^_/u` pattern
- 12 unused `setMessages` destructures in
`useCanvasSession.unit.spec.ts`: rename to `_setMessages` (helper
returns both the mock and `result`; tests only assert on `result`)
- 3 unused `handlers` destructures in `useOpenClawChat.unit.spec.ts`:
dropped from destructuring
- 1 unused `callbacks` destructure in `useSSEStream.unit.spec.ts`:
dropped
- 4 stale `// eslint-disable-next-line
@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` directives that had no matching
warning (auto-fixed by `eslint --fix`)

Zero behavior change, zero test coverage change — purely removing dead
bindings.

## Follow-up

Next PR will:
1. Promote `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` from `warn` to `error` in
the tests/ override (`web/eslint.config.mjs:458-466`)
2. Keep `--quiet` in the lint script (other warning-level rules would
otherwise flood CI logs)

Tracking #1073.

## Test plan

- [x] `npx eslint tests/ --rule '{"@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any":
"off"}'` — 0 warnings, 0 errors (was 23+4)
- [x] `pnpm --filter web test:unit useCanvasSession useOpenClawChat
useSSEStream useArtifactsSidebar useMattermostIntegration GeneralTab
PaywallContent` — 160/160 pass
- [x] `pnpm --filter web lint` — clean

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
test(web): OpenClawThread 全面覆盖 (#894 Step 3 补) (#1107)

## Summary

Epic #894 Step 3 (#897) 剩下的最后一个文件。其余 5 个 Step 3
文件（OpenClawAssistantMessage / OpenClawUserMessage / useArtifactsSidebar
/ useMattermostIntegration / useOpenClawRuntime）都已经有测试 — 本 PR 把
\`OpenClawThread.tsx\` (380 LOC) 从 0% 补到全分支。

## 新增 30 个测试

| 分组 | 数量 | 覆盖点 |
|---|---|---|
| historyLoaded / awaitingGreeting 门控 | 5 | Empty 分支 / "Setting things
up" 横幅 / compact 变体 bypass |
| Load older messages | 4 | hasMore / loadingMore disabled / 点击回调 |
| QuickActionCards 可见性 | 8 | 8 条显示门控全覆盖（messageCount / historyLoaded /
awaitingGreeting / thread.isRunning / compact / onSendMessage /
showQuickActions / agentId 透传）|
| UserMessageFactory | 4 | \`__SESSION_DIVIDER__\` → SessionDivider 分流 /
isLastMessage / compact 透传 |
| AssistantMessageFactory | 5 | isConsecutive 4 种组合（首条 / 前条 user / 前条
assistant / 前条 tool-group）+ props 透传 |
| auto-scroll 副作用 | 2 | 新 user 消息 → scrollTo instant / 新 assistant 不触发 |
| ScrollToBottomButton | 2 | 阈值下隐藏 / 阈值上显示+点击滚动 |

## Harness 要点

**\`@assistant-ui/react\` mock**：\`useMessage()\` 需要 per-message 上下文，用
\`React.createContext\` 模拟 — 模块级变量会被 lazy JSX eval 覆盖成最后一次迭代值（classic
closure pitfall，已踩过一次）。

**jsdom \`scrollTo\` 缺失**：\`HTMLElement.scrollTo\` 在 jsdom 里没实现；用
\`Object.defineProperty(el, 'scrollTo', { configurable: true, value:
vi.fn() })\` 手搭，不能用 \`vi.spyOn\`（spyOn 要求属性存在）。

**子组件 stub**：OpenClawUserMessage / OpenClawAssistantMessage /
QuickActionCards 都 stub 成 testid + 数据属性，让 factory 透传逻辑能够直接 snapshot。

## Bug-hunting

无新发现。ackActive / isConsecutive / isLastMessage 计算逻辑都合理；auto-scroll 只在新
user 消息上触发（正确）；ScrollToBottomButton 的 200px 阈值 + scrollHeight
判可滚动的条件都合理。

## Test plan

- [x] 30/30 OpenClawThread 测试通过
- [x] \`npx tsc --noEmit\` clean
- [x] \`pnpm lint\` clean
- [ ] CI 绿

## 关联

- Epic #894 Step 3 (#897)
- Step 3 所有文件覆盖后 #897 可 close（5 个已测 + OpenClawThread 本 PR）

| AssistantMessageFactory | 5 | isConsecutive 4 种组合（首条 / 前条 user / 前条
assistant / 前条 tool-group）+ props 透传 |
| auto-scroll 副作用 | 2 | 新 user 消息 → scrollTo instant / 新 assistant 不触发 |
| ScrollToBottomButton | 2 | 阈值下隐藏 / 阈值上显示+点击滚动 |

## Harness 要点

**\`@assistant-ui/react\` mock**：\`useMessage()\` 需要 per-message 上下文，用
\`React.createContext\` 模拟 — 模块级变量会被 lazy JSX eval 覆盖成最后一次迭代值（classic
closure pitfall，已踩过一次）。

**jsdom \`scrollTo\` 缺失**：\`HTMLElement.scrollTo\` 在 jsdom 里没实现；用
\`Object.defineProperty(el, 'scrollTo', { configurable: true, value:
vi.fn() })\` 手搭，不能用 \`vi.spyOn\`（spyOn 要求属性存在）。

**子组件 stub**：OpenClawUserMessage / OpenClawAssistantMessage /
QuickActionCards 都 stub 成 testid + 数据属性，让 factory 透传逻辑能够直接 snapshot。

## Bug-hunting

无新发现。ackActive / isConsecutive / isLastMessage 计算逻辑都合理；auto-scroll 只在新
user 消息上触发（正确）；ScrollToBottomButton 的 200px 阈值 + scrollHeight
判可滚动的条件都合理。

## Test plan

- [x] 30/30 OpenClawThread 测试通过
- [x] \`npx tsc --noEmit\` clean
- [x] \`pnpm lint\` clean
- [ ] CI 绿

## 关联

- Epic #894 Step 3 (#897)
- Step 3 所有文件覆盖后 #897 可 close（5 个已测 + OpenClawThread 本 PR）

commit.message:
test(web): SlackSetupWizard try/finally + ChannelsSection effect deps (#1066 + #1074 part 1) (#1105)

Two non-bug hygiene items from the review follow-up queue. Small PR
intentionally — each item alone is < 10 lines, but they share the
"review follow-up polish" model, so bundling avoids churn.

## 1. SlackSetupWizard spec robustness (#1066)

- Wrap the `process.on('unhandledRejection', ...)` listener in
`try/finally` so the handler is removed even if an `expect()` above
throws. This matches the `try/finally` pattern already in place for the
fake-timer test earlier in the same file.
- Fix the `onUnhandled` signature from `(e: PromiseRejectionEvent |
Error)` to `(reason: unknown)`. Runtime behavior was fine — Node calls
the handler with `(reason, promise)` and the existing `caught.push(e)`
happened to receive `reason` — but the type was misleading to future
readers.

## 2. ChannelsSection `ChannelCard` effect deps (#1074 part 1)

- Add `onPairingExpanded` to the `useEffect` deps array; remove the
`eslint-disable-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps`.
- Parent re-renders pass a fresh closure each render, but
`autoExpandPairing` only flips true when the channel list changes, so in
the worst case this fires one extra idempotent `setPairing(true)` +
`onPairingExpanded?.()`. Both are no-ops on repeat.
- Benefit: removes a stale-closure risk for any future caller whose
callback identity is less stable than today's `onClearLastAdded`.

### Skipped: #1074 part 2 (spec file split)

Not included. Low-priority maintainability; splitting ~600-line spec
into 3–4 files would risk jscpd duplication drift (mocks/factories
replicated across files). Can revisit if the spec ever becomes painful
to debug.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm --filter web test:unit SlackSetupWizard ChannelsSection` —
79/79 pass (30 + 49)
- [x] `pnpm --filter web lint` — clean
- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` — clean

Closes #1066
Closes #1074

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(web): stop reporting MM connection errors to Sentry (#1103)

## Summary
- Removes `Sentry.logger.error/warn` calls from
`captureMMConnectionFailure` — these bypass `beforeSend` and were still
consuming 10K+ events/user/hour, overwhelming Sentry quota
- Retains breadcrumb-only reporting (free, provides context for
subsequent real errors)
- `beforeSend` filter in `sentry.client.config.ts` kept as safety net
for old cached releases
- Removes now-unused `sanitizeURL` helper and `sanitizeUrl` import

## Context
Sentry issue: https://serendipity-one-inc.sentry.io/issues/7401127622/

Previous attempts (dedup windows → Sentry Logs migration) failed because
`Sentry.logger.*` with `enableLogs: true` still creates issues and
consumes event quota, unlike `beforeSend` which only intercepts
`captureException`/`captureMessage`.

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests updated and passing (`connectionDedup.unit.spec.ts` — 6
tests)
- [x] Lint passes
- [ ] Verify on staging that the Sentry issue stops receiving new events

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -78,9 +78,9 @@ Sentry.init({
   replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0,
 
   beforeSend(event) {
-    // Drop MM connection errors — these are now reported via Sentry Logs
-    // (not captureException) in current code. Old cached releases still emit
-    // captureException events; drop them here to stop the 200K+ event storm.
+    // Drop MM connection errors — current code only records breadcrumbs, but
+    // old cached releases may still emit captureException. Keep this filter
+    // as a safety net until all clients have updated.
     const exMsg = event.exception?.values?.[0]?.value ?? ''
     if (exMsg.includes('Mattermost connection lost')) return null

@@ -6,15 +6,13 @@
  * 2. Data integrity issues (seq gaps, backfill failures, post verification)
  * 3. Bot availability (poll exhausted)
  *
- * Connection failures use Sentry Logs (not captureMessage/captureException)
- * to avoid creating issues and consuming event quota. Logs are searchable
- * in the Sentry Logs tab and designed for high-volume diagnostics.
+ * Connection failures are recorded as breadcrumbs only — no Sentry events,
+ * logs, or quota consumption. Breadcrumbs provide context if the user
+ * hits a different (real) error afterward.
  */
 
 import * as Sentry from '@sentry/nextjs'
 
-import { sanitizeUrl } from './networkMonitor'
-
 // ─── Connection Monitoring ──────────────────────────────────────────
 
 export type MMConnectionFailureReason =
@@ -38,20 +36,19 @@ const recentLogs = new Map<string, number>()
 const LOG_DEDUP_MS = 60_000
 
 /**
- * Report a Mattermost connection failure via Sentry Logs.
+ * Report a Mattermost connection failure as a breadcrumb only.
  *
- * Uses Sentry.logger (structured logs) instead of captureMessage so that
- * connection diagnostics don't create issues or consume the event quota.
- * A breadcrumb is always added for context on real issues.
+ * Sentry Logs and captureException are both disabled for MM connection
+ * errors — they produce 10K+ events/user/hour during network instability,
+ * overwhelming the Sentry quota. Breadcrumbs are free and still provide
+ * context if the user hits a different (real) error afterward.
  */
 export function captureMMConnectionFailure(ctx: MMConnectionFailureContext) {
   const now = Date.now()
   const last = recentLogs.get(ctx.reason)
   if (last && now - last < LOG_DEDUP_MS) return
   recentLogs.set(ctx.reason, now)
 
-  // Always add a breadcrumb — cheap, and provides context if the user
-  // hits a different error shortly after connection issues.
   Sentry.addBreadcrumb({
     category: 'mattermost.connection',
     message: `MM connection failure: ${ctx.reason}`,
@@ -62,18 +59,6 @@ export function captureMMConnectionFailure(ctx: MMConnectionFailureContext) {
       error_message: ctx.errorMessage,
     },
   })
-
-  const isFatal = ctx.reason === 'reconnect_exhausted' || ctx.reason === 'bot_poll_exhausted'
-  const logFn = isFatal ? Sentry.logger.error : Sentry.logger.warn
-
-  logFn(`MM connection: ${ctx.reason}`, {
-    reason: ctx.reason,
-    error_message: ctx.errorMessage ?? '',
-    reconnect_attempt: ctx.reconnectAttempt ?? 0,
-    max_reconnect_attempts: ctx.maxReconnectAttempts ?? 0,
-    server_url: sanitizeURL(ctx.serverURL) ?? '',
-    monitor: 'mattermost_connection',
-  })
 }
 
 // ─── Data Integrity Monitoring ──────────────────────────────────────
@@ -147,10 +132,3 @@ export function captureMMDataIssue(ctx: MMDataIssueContext) {
     }
   })
 }
-
-// ─── Helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-
-function sanitizeURL(url?: string): string | undefined {
-  if (!url) return undefined
-  return sanitizeUrl(url)
-}

commit.message:
feat(web): wire dependency-cruiser for import boundaries (A1-PR1, warn mode) (#1098)

## Summary
- Wires **dependency-cruiser** as the engine for W1–W6 import
architecture contracts.
- CI runs in **warning mode** (`WARN_ONLY=1`) — violations print but
don't fail the build.
- A1-PR2 freezes the baseline into an allowlist; A1-PR3 flips the hard
gate.
- Also applies review feedback on the A1 spec merged in #1094.

## Baseline (from this config)
**14 errors + 4 warnings** across the 609 modules / 1645 dependencies
cruised. Hot spots:

| Contract | Count |
|----------|-------|
| W1 (lib pure) | 2 |
| W2 (hooks pure) | 3 |
| W3 (contexts pure) | 5 |
| W4 (components below pages) | 2 |
| W5 (feature isolation, warn-level) | 4 |
| W6 (theme leaf) | 1 |

More than the ~6 grep-based estimate in the spec because
dependency-cruiser also follows relative-path imports and re-exports,
which grep missed.

## Changes

### New files
- `web/.dependency-cruiser.cjs` — W1–W6 rules, proper `\[locale\]` regex
escaping, tsconfig resolver
- `web/scripts/ci-lint/00-run-all.sh` — CI-lint orchestrator (mirrors
`services/claw-interface/scripts/ci-lint/` layout)
- `web/scripts/ci-lint/01-import-boundaries.sh` — dep-cruiser runner
with `WARN_ONLY` switch

### Modified
- `web/package.json` — `lint:imports` / `lint:ci` scripts,
`dependency-cruiser` devDep
- `.github/workflows/code-quality.yml` — new "Import boundaries check
(warn mode, A1-PR1)" step in `web-quality` job
- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-web-import-boundaries.md` — applies
review feedback from #1094 (see below)

## #1094 review feedback addressed
1. **W1 extended to forbid `lib → theme`** (Option A per decision).
`seo.ts → brand-assets` becomes a real W1 violation, fixable in A1-PR4+
by moving brand-assets to `src/config/brand/` or `src/lib/brand/`.
2. **`[locale]` regex pitfall** documented in spec's 风险与缓解 section and
applied to W5 patterns in the config (`\\[locale\\]`).
3. **Layer diagram** clarified with explicit `hooks → contexts`
direction annotation.

Non-blocking Codex suggestions (W5 shared-directory definition, grep
reproducibility) deferred to A1-PR2.

## Local verification
- `pnpm lint` (ESLint) — pass
- `pnpm lint:imports` (WARN_ONLY=0) — exit 14, prints all 14 errors + 4
warns
- `WARN_ONLY=1 pnpm lint:ci` — exit 0, prints warnings but doesn't fail
- Config uses escaped `\\[locale\\]` and `tsConfig +
enhancedResolveOptions` — verified dep-cruiser resolves `@/*` aliases
(609 modules cruised vs earlier 2-module dry-run without resolver)

## Test plan
- [ ] CI `code-quality / web-quality` job runs new step, logs baseline
violations, does not fail build
- [ ] Existing ESLint / TS / unit-test / jscpd steps still pass
- [ ] PR-size gate passes (~180 new lines excluding lockfile)
- [ ] Reviewer to validate the W5 regex capture-group approach (`$1`
reference) works as expected vs an explicit feature-name whitelist

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -41,8 +41,8 @@ Layer 4  │ app/           (pages, features)   │  ← consumer, 可 import 
          └────────────────────────────────────┘
                        ▲           ▲
          ┌─────────────┴──┐  ┌─────┴────────────┐
-Layer 3  │ components/    │  │ hooks/, contexts/│  ← UI + state
-         └────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘
+Layer 3  │ components/    │  │ hooks/  →  contexts/│  ← UI + state
+         └────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘  (hooks may import contexts; not vice versa)
                        ▲           ▲
          ┌─────────────┴───────────┴────────────┐
 Layer 2  │ lib/           (API, utils, auth)    │  ← domain utilities
@@ -53,13 +53,16 @@ Layer 1  │ theme/, config/, utils/                │  ← leaf primitives
          └────────────────────────────────────────┘
 ```
 
+**Layer 3 方向:** `hooks/ → contexts/` 允许 (W2);`contexts/ → hooks/` 禁止 (W3)。两者虽在同层但非对称。
+
 **注:** `src/pages/` 仅剩 legacy `_error.tsx` (Next.js 12 pages router 残留),在合约里归入 `app` 层或单独豁免。
 
 ## 合约 (W1–W6)
 
-### W1 — `lib/` 不依赖 UI 层
-`src/lib/**` 禁止 import `src/components/**` / `src/hooks/**` / `src/contexts/**` / `src/app/**`。
+### W1 — `lib/` 不依赖 UI 层或 theme
+`src/lib/**` 禁止 import `src/components/**` / `src/hooks/**` / `src/contexts/**` / `src/app/**` / `src/theme/**`。
 `lib/` 是业务工具层,应纯粹基于 data / API / 原生 JS 逻辑。
+**为什么也禁 `theme/`**: theme 是 Layer 1 leaf(见 W6),语义上只含 token / brand 定义。若 `lib/` 需要业务化的 "brand assets"(如 `getActiveBrandAssets`),这些资源属于**业务配置**而非纯 token,应搬到 `src/config/brand/` 或 `src/lib/brand/`。保持 theme 的单向 leaf 性质让两端都更干净。
 
 ### W2 — `hooks/` 不依赖 UI
 `src/hooks/**` 禁止 import `src/components/**` / `src/app/**`。

 | 合约 | 违规数 | 违规文件 |
 |------|--------|----------|
-| W1 (lib 纯净) | 2 | `src/lib/payment/handlePaymentSuccess.ts` → `@/hooks/useBillingCredits`;`src/lib/seo.ts` → `@/theme/brand-assets` |
+| W1 (lib 纯净) | 2 | `src/lib/payment/handlePaymentSuccess.ts` → `@/hooks/useBillingCredits`;`src/lib/seo.ts` → `@/theme/brand-assets`(后者按修正后的 W1 含 `theme/` 确实违规) |
 | W2 (hooks 纯净) | 2 | `src/hooks/useBrandVocabulary.ts` → `@/components/BrandThemeProvider`;`src/hooks/useRequireChat.ts` → `@/components/onboarding/OnboardingProvider` |
 | W3 (contexts 纯净) | 0 | ✅ 已干净 |
 | W4 (components 不跨 app) | (待 A1-PR1 扫描,预计 ≤20) | — |
@@ -123,9 +126,9 @@ module.exports = {
     {
       name: 'W1-lib-pure',
       severity: 'error',
-      comment: 'lib/ must not import UI layers — see 2026-04-20-web-import-boundaries.md',
+      comment: 'lib/ must not import UI layers or theme — see 2026-04-20-web-import-boundaries.md',
       from: { path: '^src/lib' },
-      to: { path: '^src/(components|hooks|contexts|app)' },
+      to: { path: '^src/(components|hooks|contexts|app|theme)' },
     },
     // W2, W3, W4, W5, W6 同上
   ],
@@ -169,6 +172,17 @@ fi
 Dry-run 阶段发现 dependency-cruiser 默认不展开 `tsconfig.paths` 的 `@/*` → `src/*`(违规统计归零)。
 **缓解:** A1-PR1 在 `.dependency-cruiser.cjs` 显式传 `tsConfig + enhancedResolveOptions`;如果仍不 work, fallback 到 `eslint-plugin-boundaries`。Spec 不押宝工具最终形态。
 
+### `[locale]` 目录名 regex 陷阱
+Dependency-cruiser 的 `from.path` / `to.path` 值是**正则表达式**,不是 glob。`src/app/[locale]/admin` 里的 `[locale]` 会被当作字符类 `[aelco]`,默认匹配到 `src/app/a/admin` 之类不存在的路径 —— 合约**静默失配**、0 违规假绿。
+**缓解:** A1-PR1 配置中所有涉及 `[locale]` 的路径写成 `\\[locale\\]`:
+```js
+// 正确
+from: { path: '^src/app/\\[locale\\]/admin' },
+// 错误(会被当字符类)
+from: { path: '^src/app/[locale]/admin' },
+```
+A1-PR1 验证项中加"故意写违规看是否被检出",作为活体 smoke test。
+
 ### Next.js `app/` 特殊符号
 `src/app/[locale]/page.tsx` 等 Next.js 文件约定:`page.tsx` / `layout.tsx` / `loading.tsx` / `error.tsx` 在 path 语义上 leaf,合约中归入 `app` 层 consumer。Server Components 和 Client Components 的 `"use client"` 边界不影响 import 路径分析,dependency-cruiser 不做跨运行时分析。

@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+/**
+ * dependency-cruiser config — web/ import architecture contracts W1-W6
+ *
+ * Contracts are specified in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-web-import-boundaries.md.
+ *
+ * IMPORTANT — `path` values are regex, NOT glob. The `[locale]` segment in
+ * the Next.js App Router must be escaped as `\\[locale\\]` or it will be
+ * interpreted as the character class `[aelco]` and match the wrong paths.
+ */
+module.exports = {
+  forbidden: [
+    {
+      name: 'W1-lib-pure',
+      severity: 'error',
+      comment:
+        'lib/ must not import UI layers or theme. lib/ is Layer 2 domain utilities; ' +
+        'components/hooks/contexts/app are above, theme/ is a parallel leaf. ' +
+        'See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-web-import-boundaries.md',
+      from: { path: '^src/lib' },
+      to: { path: '^src/(components|hooks|contexts|app|theme)' },
+    },
+    {
+      name: 'W2-hooks-pure',
+      severity: 'error',
+      comment:
+        'hooks/ must not import UI (components) or pages (app). ' +
+        'hooks may depend on lib/ and contexts/.',
+      from: { path: '^src/hooks' },
+      to: { path: '^src/(components|app)' },
+    },
+    {
+      name: 'W3-contexts-pure',
+      severity: 'error',
+      comment:
+        'contexts/ must not import components/hooks/app. ' +
+        'Contexts define state — they should not consume UI or hooks.',
+      from: { path: '^src/contexts' },
+      to: { path: '^src/(components|hooks|app)' },
+    },
+    {
+      name: 'W4-components-below-pages',
+      severity: 'error',
+      comment:
+        'components/ must not import src/app/ — reusable UI should not depend on specific pages/features.',
+      from: { path: '^src/components' },
+      to: { path: '^src/app' },
+    },
+    {
+      name: 'W5-feature-isolation',
+      severity: 'warn',
+      comment:
+        'Features under src/app/[locale]/X/ must not import src/app/[locale]/Y/ (X != Y). ' +
+        'Share via src/components or src/lib. Baseline violations frozen in A1-PR2; ' +
+        'this rule stays `warn` until A1-PR3 flips the hard gate.',
+      from: { path: '^src/app/\\[locale\\]/([^/]+)/' },
+      to: {
+        path: '^src/app/\\[locale\\]/([^/]+)/',
+        pathNot: '^src/app/\\[locale\\]/$1/',
+      },
+    },
+    {
+      name: 'W6-theme-leaf',
+      severity: 'error',
+      comment:
+        'theme/ is a Layer 1 leaf — must not import any business code. ' +
+        'theme contains only token / brand primitives.',
+      from: { path: '^src/theme' },
+      to: { path: '^src/(lib|hooks|components|contexts|app|utils|config)' },
+    },
+  ],
+  options: {
+    tsPreCompilationDeps: true,
+    tsConfig: { fileName: 'tsconfig.json' },
+    enhancedResolveOptions: {
+      exportsFields: ['exports'],
+      conditionNames: ['import', 'require', 'node', 'default'],
+      mainFields: ['main', 'module'],
+    },
+    doNotFollow: { path: 'node_modules' },
+    exclude: {
+      path: '(node_modules|\\.next|coverage|tests|__tests__|\\.test\\.|\\.spec\\.)',
+    },
+  },
+}

 		"app:prod": "APP_ENV=production dotenv -e .env.production -- next build && rm -rf out/alternatives out/alternatives-seo",
 		"start": "next start",
 		"lint": "eslint src/ tests/ --quiet",
+		"lint:imports": "bash scripts/ci-lint/01-import-boundaries.sh",
+		"lint:ci": "bash scripts/ci-lint/00-run-all.sh",
 		"dup:src": "jscpd -c .jscpd.src.json",
 		"dup:tests": "jscpd -c .jscpd.tests.json",
 		"dup": "pnpm dup:src && pnpm dup:tests",
@@ -77,6 +79,7 @@
 		"@vitest/coverage-v8": "^4.0.18",
 		"@vitest/expect": "^4.0.18",
 		"cross-env": "^7.0.3",
+		"dependency-cruiser": "^17.3.10",
 		"dotenv": "^17.2.3",
 		"dotenv-cli": "^7.4.2",
 		"eslint": "^9.39.2",

@@ -0,0 +1,23 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# web/scripts/ci-lint/00-run-all.sh
+#
+# Runs every web/ CI quality gate script in sequence. Fails on the first
+# script that fails. Mirrors services/claw-interface/scripts/ci-lint/ layout.
+#
+# Env:
+#   WARN_ONLY=1   Pass through to child scripts that support warning mode.
+#                 Useful during rollout of a new gate. Remove once all gates
+#                 are hard-enforced.
+set -euo pipefail
+cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.."
+
+scripts=(
+  "scripts/ci-lint/01-import-boundaries.sh"
+)
+
+for script in "${scripts[@]}"; do
+  echo "▶ $script"
+  bash "$script"
+done
+
+echo "✓ All web/ ci-lint checks passed"

@@ -0,0 +1,29 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# web/scripts/ci-lint/01-import-boundaries.sh
+#
+# Enforces W1-W6 import architecture contracts via dependency-cruiser.
+# Config: web/.dependency-cruiser.cjs
+# Spec:   docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-web-import-boundaries.md
+#
+# Env:
+#   WARN_ONLY=1   Do not fail the build on violations; emit GitHub Actions
+#                 warning and exit 0. Used during A1 rollout (PR1..PR2).
+#                 A1-PR3 removes this branch (hard gate).
+set -euo pipefail
+cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.."
+
+WARN_ONLY="${WARN_ONLY:-0}"
+
+CMD=(pnpm exec dependency-cruiser
+  --config .dependency-cruiser.cjs
+  --output-type err
+  'src/**/*.{ts,tsx}')
+
+if [ "$WARN_ONLY" = "1" ]; then
+  if ! "${CMD[@]}"; then
+    echo "::warning::import-boundaries violations detected (warning-mode during A1 rollout — see docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-web-import-boundaries.md)"
+    exit 0
+  fi
+else
+  "${CMD[@]}"
+fi

commit.message:
chore(web): remove dead verify step from TelegramSetupWizard (#1096)

## Summary

- Removes the unreachable `verify` step from `TelegramSetupWizard`
(`setStep('verify')` was never called)
- Drops the dead `pairCode`/`pairSaving` state, `handleApprovePairing`
handler, verify UI block, `approveChannelPairing` import
- Drops `verifyTitle` / `verifyDesc` i18n keys across 10 locale files
(unused after the UI block removal)
- Strips the corresponding mock + comment from the wizard's unit spec

The API-level `approveChannelPairing` is retained — it is still used by
the generic pairing flow in `useClawSettings.ts` (surfaced through
`ChannelsSection`'s inline pairing panel). `pairingCodePlaceholder` also
stays for that reason.

Fixes #1064 (part 1 of 2). Part 2 (hardcoded `allow_from: ['*']` while
the `allowlist` policy is still offered) is left open pending a product
decision on whether `allowlist` mode should expose a real whitelist UI
control.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm --filter web test:unit TelegramSetupWizard` — 24/24 tests
pass
- [x] `pnpm --filter web lint` — clean
- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` — clean
- [x] Verified `grep -r "verifyTitle\|verifyDesc"` returns no matches
- [x] Verified wizard happy path tests still cover welcome → create-bot
→ input-token → configuring → complete

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs(web): add dead code / dep health spec (B1) (#1095)

## Summary
- Design spec for dead code + dependency health CI gate in `web/` using
**knip**.
- First PR of the **B track** (parallel to the A track / import
boundaries via dependency-cruiser, spec merged in #1094).
- Pure doc — no code changes, no CI gates added.

## Context
Mirrors the claw-interface CI quality bar (ruff / pyright / 6
import-linter contracts / jscpd / **deptry** / coverage 90%). `web/` has
`@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` (single-file) but **no cross-file
dead code / dep health tooling**. This spec fills that gap with a single
tool (knip) covering unused exports / files / deps / devDeps / unlisted
/ duplicates / binaries.

See `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-web-dead-code.md` for:
- Tool selection rationale (knip over ts-prune + depcheck +
eslint-plugin-unused-imports)
- Baseline from `npx knip` dry-run: ~120 violations (6 files, 6 unused
deps, 3 unused devDeps, 1 unlisted dep, 1 unlisted binary, 39 unused
exports, 58 unused exported types, 6 duplicate exports)
- 2-stage rollout (spec → wire-up + baseline freeze + hard gate in one
PR)
- Category-based cleanup order (duplicates → unused deps → unused files
→ exports by module)
- FP risk areas (Playwright setup files, dynamic imports, pnpm-hoisted
transitives)

## Non-goals
- Does **not** tighten existing thresholds (complexity 25 / max-lines
500 / coverage 55% stay).
- Does **not** tighten `tsconfig` (already strict).
- Does **not** introduce ts-prune / depcheck /
eslint-plugin-unused-imports (knip covers all).

## Test plan
- [x] Markdown renders correctly
- [ ] Reviewer validates knip over three-tool composite rationale
- [ ] Reviewer reviews baseline categorization (duplicate exports in
`src/lib/api/backend.ts` are 1st target — low-risk, high signal)
- [ ] No CI gates triggered (pure doc PR)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
